summary: Rebalance Kamikakushi away from trade-dominance to MULTI-PILLAR house influence/strength; design the castle-town takeover (peaceful or otherwise) and a beefed-up national Edo multi-pillar ranking endgame. Parallel facets → synthesize → verify.

synth.rebalance_summary:
RECOMMENDED REBALANCE — one design, all §1.11/macro notes applied.

House Influence (家威 ka-i) stops being a single trade-leaning score (PRD §1.6's seven conversion levers, of which trade/meibutsu was the dominant path and the literal Edo climax). It becomes the UMBRELLA NAME for FOUR parallel, achievement-driven pillars the player grows in lockstep:
- ARMS (武威 bu-i, MARTIAL) — retainers/men-at-arms fielded, victories & clears, road/estate security held, rival houses' forces bested.
- ESTATE & WEALTH (家産 kasan, ECONOMIC) — lands/koku, treasury & creditworthiness, AND trade. Trade/meibutsu is now ONE of three sub-engines INSIDE this one pillar (the others: LAND reclamation, TREASURY/finance), so the famous product — formerly the whole endgame — is one optional capstone in one sub-engine of one of four pillars.
- STANDING & OFFICE (政威 sei-i, POLITICAL/TERRITORIAL) — villages→region→castle-town under the house's sway, domain offices & administration held, alliances sealed, rivals eclipsed.
- NAME & HONOUR (家格 kakaku, PRESTIGE/CULTURAL) — formal kakaku rank, reputation, patronage, notable deeds, lineage restored, recognition recorded in official ink.

Tiers (T0 Estate → T4 Edo, adopting the prompt's numbering; = PRD's T1→T5 offset by one — see decisions) are gated by a FOUR-VALUE per-pillar threshold with a balanced-development FLOOR: you must clear ALL FOUR minimums to advance, but a capped overflow (≤25%) lets a strong pillar partly carry a lagging one, so specialists are rewarded without ignoring a pillar. This structurally forbids a pure-trade or pure-combat tier-skip — the single most important de-trade-weighting mechanism. Early tiers weight Estate+Arms (survive, earn keep, get strong); upper tiers weight Office+Name (the takeover and the national ranking are won socially).

Each pillar maps to a distinct protagonist domain so the SPINE (a mediocre man growing only by grind across COMBAT and LABOUR/JOBS/SKILLS/TRADES) stays load-bearing: Arms←combat/weapon-skills/men-at-arms leadership (this is where D-007 'combat earns standing' lives); Estate←labour/jobs/skills/trades/crafting; Office←jobs-as-offices/administration/quests; Name←the recognition layer reflecting the other three plus deeds/patronage/lineage. Accrual stays strictly achievement+seasonal-milestone (never a time-trickle, never a flat per-action increment): every +pillar event names a diegetic deed, and the periodic award is a JUDGED RESULT of accumulated state appraised once per season, not a per-action drip.

The T3 castle-town takeover becomes a multi-route 'become the leading house' conquest. The T4 Edo climax becomes a national multi-pillar mitate-banzuke (a period-authentic parody ranking broadsheet) on which the HOUSE climbs all four columns — celebrating overall strength, not the product. D-010's honest ceiling is preserved by ranking the HOUSE, not the man; the protagonist stays its architect/right-hand. Lean discipline: side factions remain MULTIPLIERS into the four pillars (never new pillars), upper tiers stay abstract holdings/correspondence/ranking BOARDS (not walkable maps), and the full banzuke is a T3+ endgame layer (T0–T2 keep a simpler standing display). This is explicitly a T3–T4 roadmap capstone, not v1 scope (v1 = T0–T1).

pillars.what_it_is: The house's martial authority: the strength, reliability and reputation of its men-at-arms and the protagonist's own combat record, plus the security it provides. This is where the combat pillar EARNS standing (the D-007 premise), turning the first-class combat systems already specced — weapon-skill lines, mob clears, loot/found+crafted gear, the men-at-arms leadership rungs — into house standing rather than a side activity.
pillars.how_it_grows: Achievement JUMPS on recognised martial deeds + seasonal RESULTS appraising standing force/security, never per-kill trickle. The existing Combat-Deeds discipline (per-event caps, ~0.1-level/kill) carries over so no single fight spikes the pillar. Jumps: a bandit nest cleared and the road 'declared safe' in the books; a rival's enforcer arm (Hanzaki/the racket muscle) broken; rival retainers bested in a sanctioned dispute/duel; a security crisis quelled. Seasonal results: 'the men-at-arms held the quarter through the lean season with no losses'; a standing patrol kept. Feeders: the Matagi hunters and drill-yard reputation multiply martial accrual.
pillars.example_milestones: T0: survive the humbling first fight; clear the first pest/animal nest; stand a held night-watch. T1: a valley road declared safe; 2-3 men-at-arms on a rota; the grain store defended. T2: a bandit nest cleared; the high-pass route secured; a standing patrol. T3: the house is the seat's de-facto road-security; a rival's enforcer arm broken; the security-crisis set-piece won (the assertive route to a domain office). T4: a domain-defence/security commendation travels up to Edo; the Hanzaki duel finally WON (survived all game, beatable on labour-built endurance); a frontier held through a famine-winter.

pillars.pillar: ESTATE & WEALTH — 家産 (kasan) — ECONOMIC (trade is ONE of three sub-engines here)
pillars.what_it_is: The house's material base: lands held & reclaimed (koku), treasury/creditworthiness, AND trade. Critically, trade is demoted to ONE of THREE economic sub-engines — (a) LAND (shinden reclamation, the classic goshi-advancement lever), (b) TREASURY/FINANCE (debt→solvency→creditworthiness, goyokin), (c) TRADE (routes, broker standing, the optional meibutsu). The famous product that used to BE the endgame is now one capstone option in sub-engine (c) of this single pillar.
pillars.how_it_grows: Achievement JUMPS + seasonal harvest/audit RESULTS. Jumps: the first shinden plot's yield recorded; the kura declared solvent; a usurious loan restructured into creditworthiness; the meibutsu graded; the Kuzuhara embankment securing yield. Seasonal: 'this week's/season's harvest exceeded quota'; 'the autumn audit records the surplus'. A judged result of accumulated state — flooding a good or grinding kills posts nothing until the season's books acknowledge it. Feeders: Origin trade-ties multiply the trade sub-engine; Scholars improve audit/finance efficiency. HARD CAP: trade is tuned to at most ~1/3 of this one pillar, so a pure-trade player maxes ~1/3 of one of four pillars and is hard-stopped at every tier gate by the other three.
pillars.example_milestones: T0: first shinden plot's yield recorded; kura solvent; first season's quota met. T1: cash-crop/proto-industry online; the mon on village contracts; a tradeable surplus. T2: branch holdings; the Kuzuhara embankment; broker/warehouse standing. T3: the seat's indispensable creditor/supplier; the domain's strangling debt restructured via goyokin so the daimyo's finance depends on the house (the economic route to the kanjo-kata office). T4: the meibutsu graded and celebrated in Edo markets (the OLD climax, now ONE column); restructured debt becomes national creditworthiness; origin-father Kuranosuke's debt bought back, crowning the trade sub-engine.

pillars.pillar: STANDING & OFFICE — 政威 (sei-i) — POLITICAL / TERRITORIAL
pillars.what_it_is: Governing authority: villages→region→castle-town under the house's sway, the domain OFFICES & administration it holds, alliances sealed, and rival houses politically eclipsed. This is the win-condition substrate of the T3 castle-town takeover and the pillar fed by JOBS-AS-OFFICES and quests. (Note: 政威 sei-i is a coined compound; an attested alternative is 領 ryo / 'territorial sway' — flag for the §4/§5 authenticity-harden.)
pillars.how_it_grows: Achievement JUMPS on offices granted, territory secured, alliances sealed, rivals subordinated — never trickle. Jumps: named on the household roster; granted the home-fields bailiff (jito-dai) duty; the headman cedes the granary's administration; a village dispute arbitrated; an appointment posted by the daikan; a neighbouring valley allied; a rival's office reassigned to the house. The protagonist holds a deputy/right-hand post UNDER offices held in the house's/head's name (preserving D-010 per-office). Feeders: Scholars (administration), the Neighbouring-Valleys bloc, allied retainers.
pillars.example_milestones: T0: named on the household roster; granted the home-fields bailiff (jito-dai) duty. T1: the headman's regard won; the house arbitrates a village dispute; first errand-authority beyond the gate. T2: 2+ valleys allied/anchored; a district standing roll-up. T3: domain offices held (daikan's favour → kanjo-kata recording → a magistracy-adjacent post); the 2+ rival houses eclipsed; the house tops the DOMAIN banzuke. T4: a documented merit-appointment flows DOWN through the lord; the house leads the domain's retainer houses on the national chart's political column.

pillars.pillar: NAME & HONOUR — 家格 (kakaku) — PRESTIGE / CULTURAL
pillars.what_it_is: The house's rank, reputation and recognition: formal kakaku standing, patronage given/received, notable deeds publicly recorded, lineage restored & surpassed, the daimyo's favour, and the recognition recorded in official ink. It reflects the other three pillars PLUS authored deeds — the pillar that turns substance into reckoned fame and is the soft path to the apex offices and the banzuke's upper ranks.
pillars.how_it_grows: Achievement JUMPS on recognised honour-events + seasonal reputation appraisal. Jumps: the lord's explicit recognition; the house off the foreclosure list; a sponsored festival/shrine repair; the touring-inspector's report names the house; a marriage/adoption alliance; a kakaku merit-elevation (karo-kaku); a recognised treatise on the house's water-works; the house first printed on the national banzuke. Feeders: Pilgrimage/temple and Scholars multiply prestige; the daimyo's confidence is its own head-to-head meter vs each rival. Per the §1.13 guardrail, karo-kaku/'adoption into the house name' stays aspirational narration; the protagonist's plausible personal top is chief steward / the lord's right hand.
pillars.example_milestones: T0: the lord's explicit recognition; the house off the foreclosure list. T1: a sponsored festival/shrine repair; the valley calls it 'the Kurosawa works'. T2: the embankment recognised as distinguished service; a head-temple's vouch. T3: a marriage/adoption alliance; a kakaku merit-elevation; the daimyo elevates the Kurosawa over the hereditary house. T4: the touring-inspector's report mints the top prestige step; the house named and climbing on the national mitate-banzuke; a celebrated patronage/cultural deed recorded in Edo.

synth.castle_town_takeover:
THE T3 CASTLE-TOWN TAKEOVER — 'peacefully or otherwise', honest within the bakufu.

WHAT 'TAKEOVER' AUTHENTICALLY MEANS (the plausibility wall made into the design): under the bakuhan order a goshi house cannot militarily storm a castle-town — open force = rebellion = bakufu attainder = the house destroyed. So 'take over' = becoming the DOMINANT/LEADING HOUSE of the domain: holding the commanding administrative OFFICES, owning the daimyo's confidence, and reducing the 2+ rival houses to subordinate/allied/ruined status. The castle stays the daimyo's; the Kurosawa come to RUN it for him. Historical anchors license this exactly: Yoshida Toyo of Tosa rose from district magistrate to karo on the daimyo's favour + reform competence and effectively directed the domain, displacing the hereditary faction; O-Ie Sodo (御家騒動, 'house strife') is the period's own name for factional struggle over offices/succession; and with the daimyo absent on sankin-kotai, the jodai-karo and bugyo offices WERE the de-facto government — capturing them IS capturing the domain.

THE PRIZE — the office ladder physically seated in the castle-town, each tagged to the pillar(s) that win it and the rival who holds it: DAIKAN/gun-bugyo (district admin & tax — Office+Estate; the canon Daikan/Tedai-Kuroiwa cluster sits here, the first office the house holds); KANJO-KATA (domain finance — Estate; held by Tomita's faction, won by rescuing the DOMAIN's debt); MACHI-BUGYO-sho (magistracy, law, security, the osso petition hall — Arms+Office); the senior KARO/jodai-karo seat (the apex prize, held by the OLD hereditary house by birth — the Yoshida-Toyo target — Name+Office); and the YONIN attendant-advisor (proximity to the daimyo's ear — Name, the soft path to all the others).

THE RIVAL POWER-STRUGGLE (O-Ie Sodo at goshi scale, two archetypes so the effort>talent thesis lands twice): HOUSE TOMITA (canon) = the MODERN rival — capital, connections, ruthlessness; holds finance/commercial offices and the daikan's favour; beaten by OUT-COMPETING (substance vs bought advantage). NEW HOUSE OKABE = the OLD hereditary-conservative house — birth-proud, complacent; holds the senior karo seat by lineage not merit; beaten by OUT-PERFORMING. Each rival is a contested set of meters (office-holds, daimyo-favour, allied valleys/brokers) flipped pillar by pillar. NON-LETHAL resolutions per house: DETENTE (negotiated power-share), ALLIANCE (marriage/adoption absorbs their strength), CLEAN DEFEAT (out-competed/out-performed until the daimyo reassigns their office to the Kurosawa), or DISGRACE (only via the optional racket-evidence thread — exposed and stripped of office, honouring partial justice). The player never assassinates: inverting the historical Toyo (assassinated by extremists he beat), the Kurosawa are the clean reformers who SURVIVE by daylight, and a desperate rival's resort to violence/coercion is what DISCREDITS them before the off-stage metsuke (the rebellion guardrail dramatised).

MULTIPLE PATHS (all valid, combinable, each leaning a pillar). PEACEFUL: (A) ADMINISTRATIVE/REFORMER (Office, the Yoshida-Toyo path) — distinguished service (the Kuzuhara flood-control, famine-relief, a clean audit) earns appointment daikan→kanjo-kata→toward karo; install Kurosawa allies in vacated posts. (B) ECONOMIC LEVERAGE (Estate) — rescue the DOMAIN's strangling debt via goyokin + honest rice-finance until it cannot function without the house. (C) MARRIAGE/ADOPTION ALLIANCE (Name+Office) — a sanctioned tie leaps a rung or absorbs a rival by merger. (D) DAIMYO'S FAVOUR (Name) — the yonin path: win the lord's personal confidence so he ELEVATES the Kurosawa over the hereditary house. ASSERTIVE / 'OR OTHERWISE' (pressure within bounds, never rebellion): (E) MARTIAL/SECURITY LEVERAGE (Arms) — the house's retainers + the protagonist's combat become the seat's de-facto security (put down a bandit surge/riot the rival house FUMBLES, guard the kaido, defeat a rival's retainers in sanctioned bouts), making the house the one the daimyo relies on for order. (F) COERCION/LEVERAGE within bounds — call in a rival's debts, expose their racket complicity, exploit a crisis where they fail and the Kurosawa deliver.

WIN CONDITION: a DOMAIN STANDING roll-up (the T3 face of the four-pillar model) — the Kurosawa hold a majority/plurality of the offices OR specifically the karo seat, the daimyo's confidence exceeds the leading rival's, and rivals are subordinated; completion fires when the house TOPS THE DOMAIN-LEVEL BANZUKE (a T3 precursor to the national one). Because offices map to different pillars, a pillar-heavy player still reaches 'leading' by a different office-door — three named victory shapes give replay flavour: THE STEWARD-DOMAIN (won on administrative reform + offices), THE BANKER-DOMAIN (won on finance indispensability), THE SHIELD-DOMAIN (won on security/martial trust).

SET-PIECES (so the conquest FEELS real, each drawing a different pillar): (1) THE OFFICE APPOINTMENT — the daimyo names the Kurosawa to an office over the rivals' objection in the castle audience hall (the first time the house sits where a rival sat). (2) THE OSSO/PETITION SHOWDOWN — the machi-bugyo-sho case where the racket/rival is challenged on documented proof. (3) THE SECURITY CRISIS — a bandit surge/flood/riot the rival fumbles and the Kurosawa's retainers + the protagonist resolve (the assertive route's headline, the rare dangerous fight combat earns). (4) THE GOYOKIN/DEBT-RESCUE — the domain's finances fail, the Kurosawa post the loan no rival can. (5) THE ALLIANCE / O-IE-SODO RESOLUTION — a marriage absorbs a rival, OR the daimyo arbitrates and reassigns offices, OR a rival over-reaches and discredits itself. (6) THE METSUKE/INSPECTION as legitimacy test — the touring-inspector judges WHICH house best runs the domain, bridging T3→T4.

AUTHENTICITY GUARDRAILS (must stay guardrails, not footnotes): leading house, NEVER the lord; offices not the castle; pressure not rebellion; rivals subordinated/allied/disgraced, never killed by the player.
synth.edo_ranking_endgame:
THE T4 NATIONAL EDO CLIMAX — a multi-pillar mitate-banzuke (replaces 'the meibutsu celebrated in Edo').

The climax is no longer the famous product reaching Edo's markets (the trade-weighting the human wants gone). It is the HOUSE climbing a NATIONAL, Edo-level RANKING CHART — a mitate-banzuke, the period-authentic parody-ranking broadsheet genre (cf. the chojya-banzuke 'ranking of the rich'; charts ranked rich men, restaurants, even disasters — so a 'banzuke of provincial houses' reads as a real woodblock print, NOT a game-y leaderboard and NOT an official bakufu register, which is what keeps the D-010 ceiling intact).

FORM: a woodblock broadsheet UI — top-centre title/date, EAST and WEST columns, ranks descending Yokozuna→Ozeki→Sekiwake→Komusubi→Maegashira-1..N with calligraphy shrinking down the chart, compiler's-name margin. It has FOUR PILLAR COLUMNS (Arms / Estate & Wealth / Standing & Office / Name & Honour) plus an overall east-west standing — so the player reads four sub-bars climbing in parallel and a roll-up rank, never one number. The Kurosawa start UNRANKED — printed only in the tiny bottom-margin 'mae-zumo' names (a stinging absence mirroring the existing 'the chart that omits you' beat; the first time the house name appears ANYWHERE is itself a milestone), then appear low on their strongest column, then climb each column toward the upper provincial ranks.

THE CEILING MADE DIEGETIC: the chart's untouchable 'yokozuna' head is occupied by the great real houses as historical wallpaper (Maeda of Kaga ~1.03M koku, Shimazu of Satsuma ~729k, Date of Sendai ~626k — to be fictionalised if the 'no real place-names' guardrail wins, flagged below). A goshi house can rise to a respectable provincial Maegashira/Komusubi rank, but the Ozeki/Yokozuna slots are structurally SEALED — the wall the truly powerful built, made the chart's literal geometry (same partial-justice motif as the magistrate who escapes). De-rivalling Tomita is a banzuke OVERTAKE (your name printed above theirs), not their destruction.

THE WIN CONDITION: the Kurosawa printed as a named, UPPER-RANK house on the national mitate-banzuke — publicly reckoned a first-rank provincial house, the domain's leading family, restored AND surpassing the three-generations-ago grandeur. Recognition celebrates OVERALL strength across all four pillars, not the product. SECONDARY/BRANCH victories let the player win their way: a chart-topping ARMS rank (a house of famed retainers, with an optional 'banzuke of swordsmen' sub-chart as the martial pillar's public face), an OFFICE rank (the domain's administrative power), or a NAME rank (a celebrated cultural patron-house) — each a distinct named final standing, replayability without a reset. The win is enforced numerically by the roll-up being min/geometric-mean-weighted (the weakest pillar drags hardest), so a trade-maxed, martial/political-weak house sits MID-chart and cannot reach a leading rank — 'grow all four' is the only path up.
endgame_goals_and_set_pieces: THE FOUR-ACT MULTI-PILLAR FINALE (the spine of T4): a deliberate push to the top of the banzuke spread across all the protagonist's domains so it can't be brute-forced on one pillar. ACT 1 — THE FRONTIER SECURED (Arms/leadership): as captain of retainers, lead the men-at-arms to break the brigand/Hanzaki roost and stand the climactic siege/raid-defence of the estate (dent-not-destroy on failure, never a wipe). ACT 2 — THE PETITION & THE ECLIPSE (Office/skills): mount the osso evidence-petition against the racket (ledgers, surveying, letters, gathered evidence) and politically eclipse the last rival house; partial justice — the daikan answers, the Edo laundering factor escapes. ACT 3 — THE MEIBUTSU & THE PURSE (Estate/trades): the graded meibutsu reaches its top category-rank in Nihonbashi and restructured debt becomes national creditworthiness (one strong pillar, NOT the whole climax). ACT 4 — THE SURVEY & THE LISTING (Name + combine): the Touring-Inspector Survey judges the whole house — all four pillars become 'the answer to one exam' — a favourable report travels UP through the lord, and the house's name appears and then CLIMBS the Edo banzuke. The protagonist stands at the back of every recognition beat: the HOUSE rises, he is its architect.

THE STINGING BROADSHEET (the call to climb): Konoe/the gesakusha forwards a mitate-banzuke on which Tomita is ranked and the Kurosawa absent — the existing 'chart that omits you' beat repurposed as the endgame chart's intro and the emotional engine of the whole T4 push.

THE TOURING-INSPECTOR SURVEY (the judged capstone that READS all four pillars at once): the inspector weighs fields (Estate), order/security (Arms), offices/administration (Office), and the people's condition + meibutsu + notable deeds (Name) — the capstone literally scores every domain the MC built; a neglected pillar visibly drags the report (recoverable dent, never a wipe). This is the bridge from T3 'leading house of the domain' into the T4 national chart, and the prestige pillar's top step.

THE SECURITY-CRISIS / DUEL-OF-HOUSES (the combat pillar's domain-scale payoff): a sanctioned martial contest vs a rival's retainers and/or a bandit surge or riot the rival house fumbles and the Kurosawa quell — the rare dangerous fight that EARNS a security office and a martial banzuke column, keeping combat first-class at domain scale rather than decorative.

ONE CAPSTONE CHALLENGE PER PILLAR (each a true mountaintop, optional-but-iconic): ARMS — the Hanzaki DUEL finally won (survived-not-won all game, beatable on labour-built endurance) + a frontier held through a famine-winter. ESTATE/TRADES — the meibutsu graded #1 in its category + the Kuranosuke/origin-father redemption (his debt bought back with resources the SON earned; the origin caravan becomes the supply chain that carries the meibutsu to Edo). OFFICE — the osso petition won to its partial-justice limit (real personal risk on a chosen neck — protagonist / loyal ally / gimin-style martyr; win stays partial regardless) + the last rival house eclipsed/allied. NAME — a recognised treatise on the house's agronomy/water-works (the Kuzuhara embankment written up) OR a celebrated patronage, + the Oharu/lost-child resolution at Kuzuhara (the grown Tama freed and self-determining; costly, incomplete, she may not forgive).

THE THREE SIDE-THREADS PAY OFF INTO DIFFERENT PILLARS (so 'everything the MC does matters' is structurally true, none redundant): origin-father Kuranosuke → Estate/trades capstone; the racket/osso petition → Office+Name capstone (partial justice); Oharu/lost-child → Name/closure capstone.

THE NEW BANZUKE READ ALOUD (the payoff): the published chart names the Kurosawa in the upper ranks for the first time, eclipsing Tomita — the emotional climax; the protagonist watches from the back as the house name is read, D-010 honoured structurally.
synth.long_tail:
POST-CLIMAX ENGAGEMENT WITH NO RESET (D-004 reversed; the tier climb IS the ending). Four loops, all achievement/milestone-driven, all reusing built systems so no content mountain:

(1) DEFEND THE TOP SPOT. The banzuke RE-RANKS on a periodic cadence tied to the existing sankin-kotai biennial heartbeat (the upper tier already has a clock): every attendance cycle the sheet re-ranks and rivals push back. Holding a leading rank is an ongoing goal — let a pillar go cold and a rival climbs above you, recoverable, NEVER a wipe and never a decay-tax that forces grind merely to stay put (a 'keep all four pillars warm' nudge, not punishment).

(2) OPTIONAL GROUNDED SUPER-BOSSES (no magic — the hardest grounded fights): the Hanzaki roost at max difficulty, a coordinated multi-house bandit alliance raiding branch holdings, the famine-winter siege — each repeatable/escalating, gated on labour-built conditioning (honouring no-hidden-edge), dropping the game's best FOUND gear and the heaviest Arms-pillar deeds, so combat stays a first-class endgame pillar.

(3) PER-PILLAR MASTERY GOALS: max every weapon/labour/craft skill line, complete the bestiary, grade every meibutsu variant, reclaim every plot, secure every road, hold every office, restore every named drowned — long lists of small achievement-driven dopamine that each tick a pillar column even post-climax (the incremental long tail without any reset).

(4) THE ESTATE AS SANDBOX (re-homing the old reset's teaching/management layer onto NPCs, per locked direction): the fully-grown estate becomes a teaching/management playground — Tokujiro and recruited origin friends are now taught 'from zero' (a visible mirror of the protagonist's own climb — 'a man who climbed once now starting others from zero'), and the player tunes the standing patrol/garrison, branch holdings, and meibutsu quarter as a living machine.

Lean discipline: all long-tail is escalation/mastery over what's already built — combat tables, idle producers, the banzuke meter — not new content. Upper tiers stay abstract holdings/correspondence/ranking BOARDS, never walkable maps, per the §1.13 scope guard.
synth.d010_reconciliation:
HOW IT RECONCILES WITH / STRAINS D-010 (the locked indirect/mediated Edo ceiling: the protagonist never personally becomes hatamoto, is never received by the shogun, no rags-to-daimyo).

NOTE on status: D-010 is referenced in PRD §1.14 as the 'Cap the Edo ceiling' call but is NOT yet written into docs/history/decisions.md (the log ends at D-006). So this redesign must be locked together WITH the D-010 ADR (and with D-007 'combat earns standing' and the D-004 reversal, both also authorized-but-unwritten — the Arms pillar's entire premise depends on D-007). All three should graduate to ADRs in the same lock.

HOW IT'S PRESERVED BY DEFAULT: the design keeps D-010 intact by making the HOUSE what rises, is ranked, and is recognised — the Kurosawa NAME climbs the banzuke; the protagonist is its named architect/right-hand whose service lifts the LORD'S standing (the existing lord-as-proxy / sankin-kotai mediated mechanism does the lifting). Recognition arrives mediated (through the rusui Konoe, down through Lord Munenori) and is RECORDED about the house. Three structural locks hold the wall: (a) the banzuke is a POPULAR mitate/parody broadsheet of houses (cultural fame), NOT an official bakufu status register; (b) the castle-town 'takeover' = becoming the leading/dominant house and holding domain (han-level) OFFICES, NOT bakufu rank and NOT military conquest; (c) every Edo-side recognition routes DOWN through the lord — the protagonist provisions the man who is seen, never is seen. The protagonist's personal ceiling stays chief steward / the lord's right hand (karo-kaku kept as aspirational narration only, per §1.13).

WHERE THE AMBITION STRAINS D-010 (flagged honestly, three strain points): (i) a goshi house 'dominating the castle-town' and 'topping a national chart' is a bigger HOUSE ascension than D-010's original 'lord's standing nudged + a modest meibutsu' — even house-level. (ii) 'Dethroning rival samurai houses' implies real status displacement at the seat. (iii) the protagonist personally HOLDING a domain office (vs holding it in the house's/lord's name as deputy) edges past 'never personally elevated', and the mediated merit-notation in bakufu ink (a house name in an official register) is the heaviest beat.

OPTIONS FOR THE HUMAN (decisive recommendation: A): 
- OPTION A (RECOMMENDED — keep D-010 intact): the banzuke is explicitly a PROVINCIAL/multi-domain mitate (parody/unofficial) chart, NOT a bakufu-national official ranking; 'dethroning rivals' = eclipsing them in local/domain standing, not displacing them from bakufu-recognised rank; the climb tops out at 'the leading house of the domain, noted nationally as a notable provincial house' (Maegashira/Komusubi, never Ozeki/Yokozuna); offices are held in the HOUSE's/head's name with the protagonist as deputy/right-hand. Astonishing for a goshi house, still short of hatamoto/daimyo — the honest wall stays.
- OPTION B (loosen slightly): allow a genuinely national mitate-banzuke (these existed for rich men nationwide) with a low house-level entry AND let the protagonist personally hold a real domain office (gun-bugyo/yonin) by merit — defensible via the Yoshida-Toyo precedent (a risen man holding office), still 'never hatamoto / never received by the shogun'. More triumphant, modestly less honest-ceiling.
- OPTION C (loosen more, least recommended): a formally raised kokudaka / officially elevated kakaku + a personal house-name elevation/adoption for the protagonist — strains the 'nobody stays a nobody' thesis.

The protagonist's personal rank stays HARD-capped (recommend R14 / chief steward) while the house's banzuke rank keeps climbing post-cap, decoupling personal vs house ascension. The human MUST pick A/B/C before the T3 office-appointment set-pieces and the T4 banzuke nodes are authored, since it sets the protagonist's exact title and the chart's scope.
synth.influence_lever_changes:
CONCRETE CHANGES TO THE §1.6 CONVERSION LEVERS (de-trade-weight, go multi-pillar).

CURRENT §1.6: House Influence is 'the single tracked macro-resource' — one number with named bands — converted via SEVEN levers: (1) shinden land reclamation, (2) cash crops & proto-industry → a famous meibutsu, (3) river & road works, (4) debt restructuring, (5) distinguished service to the domain, (6) patronage & alliance, (7) trade ties to Osaka. Trade/meibutsu is the dominant path and the literal T5 Edo climax.

CHANGE 1 — Influence becomes a COMPOSITE of four pillar scores, not one number. Rewrite the §1.6 opening: 家威 (ka-i) is retained as the UMBRELLA term for overall standing but is the visible roll-up of FOUR tracked pillars (Arms 武威 / Estate & Wealth 家産 / Standing & Office 政威 / Name & Honour 家格). The 'one number the UI-reveal is gated on' becomes 'four pillar scores plus a roll-up'; the four-bar 'house standing' panel itself becomes a UI reveal (unlocks when Influence first becomes visible, ~R5-R7/bailiff, per §1.12).

CHANGE 2 — RE-SORT the seven levers UNDER the four pillars (no lever is deleted; trade is demoted, combat is added): 
- ARMS (NEW pillar, was absent from §1.6): retainers/men-at-arms, victories & clears, security held, rival forces bested. This is the §1.6 change that most de-trade-weights — combat now feeds the macro (requires D-007). 
- ESTATE & WEALTH absorbs old levers (1) shinden, (2) cash-crops/proto-industry/meibutsu, (4) debt restructuring, (7) trade ties — but split into THREE capped sub-engines (LAND / TREASURY / TRADE), with TRADE (old levers 2+7) capped at ~1/3 of this one pillar. The meibutsu, formerly the climax, is one optional capstone in the TRADE sub-engine. 
- STANDING & OFFICE absorbs old lever (5) distinguished service-to-domain (reframed as offices held/administration) + the new territory/alliance/rival-eclipse content. 
- NAME & HONOUR absorbs old lever (6) patronage & alliance + the recognition/lineage/kakaku content. Old lever (3) river & road works splits: yield-protection → Estate, official credit/recognition → Name. 

CHANGE 3 — TIER GATES go from single bands to FOUR-VALUE thresholds with a balanced-development floor + capped (≤25%) overflow-substitution (replacing 'crossing a band is the tier-up moment'). Per-tier weighting drifts Estate+Arms early → Office+Name late. This is the structural de-trade-weight: a pure-trade run maxes ~1/3 of one of four pillars and is hard-stopped at every gate.

CHANGE 4 — ACCRUAL rule restated for all four pillars: two shapes ONLY — (A) achievement JUMPS (named diegetic deeds) and (B) periodic JUDGED RESULTS (seasonal/audit/harvest outcomes appraised once from accumulated state). Per-event caps carried from the Combat-Deeds discipline. Explicitly NOT a time-trickle, NOT a flat per-action increment (preserves the locked rule).

CHANGE 5 — DENTS go PER-PILLAR for legibility (a lost battle dents Arms; a scandal dents Name; a called debt dents Estate) — small, recoverable, never a wipe (recommend over composite dents; confirm against the 'no wipe' feel).

CHANGE 6 — SIDE FACTIONS stay MULTIPLIERS into specific pillars, never new pillars (so the macro stays four columns, never twelve): recommend Scholars→Estate(finance)+Office(admin); Pilgrimage/temple→Name; Origin trade-ties→Estate(trade sub-engine); Matagi→Arms. The locked '~halves time-to-tier when side factions woven in' budget is distributed across pillars, not piled on one.

CHANGE 7 — ENFORCE de-trade-weighting in the GENERATED balance tables, not just prose: cap Estate at ~1/4 of roll-up weight and trade at ~1/3 of Estate; make the roll-up closer to a min/geometric-mean than a sum; generate weights into docs/ so it's auditable (single-source-of-truth convention) and headlessly regression-test time-to-tier for pure-combat / pure-trade / balanced play.

decisions_to_lock.question: D-010 reconciliation: keep the indirect/mediated Edo ceiling intact, or loosen it for the bigger 'takeover + national ranking' ambition?
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: OPTION A — keep D-010 intact: a PROVINCIAL/mitate (parody/unofficial) banzuke, not a bakufu-national official register; 'dethroning rivals' = eclipsing them in domain standing, not displacing bakufu rank; cap at 'leading house of the domain, noted nationally as a notable provincial house'; offices held in the house's/head's name with the protagonist as deputy/right-hand; personal rank hard-capped at chief steward.
decisions_to_lock.why: It delivers the human's bigger ambition (takeover + national chart) while preserving the locked honest ceiling and the 'effort takes a nobody astonishingly far, then meets a wall the powerful built' thesis. This is the single load-bearing call — it sets the protagonist's exact title and the chart's scope, and must be made before T3 office-appointment and T4 banzuke nodes are authored.

decisions_to_lock.question: Write D-007 ('combat earns standing') and the D-004 reversal as ADRs in the same lock as this redesign?
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Yes — lock D-007, the D-004 reversal, and the D-010 ADR together with the new multi-pillar §1.6/§1.11 ADR.
decisions_to_lock.why: The Arms pillar's entire premise (combat feeds the macro) depends on D-007, which §1.13 flags as authorized-but-not-yet-written and which contradicts the still-live old 'combat is never a source of standing' framing. Without these ADRs the multi-pillar model isn't canon and the docs read as contradictory.

decisions_to_lock.question: The four-pillar set and names: are Arms / Estate & Wealth / Standing & Office / Name & Honour right, and what are the diegetic kanji vs UI labels?
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Lock the four pillars; keep authentic kanji as flavour (武威 bu-i / 家産 kasan / 政威 sei-i / 家格 kakaku) with plain English UI labels (Arms / Estate & Wealth / Standing & Office / Name & Honour). Flag 政威 sei-i as coined — confirm vs an attested alternative (領 ryo / 'territorial sway') at the §4/§5 authenticity-harden.
decisions_to_lock.why: Four pillars cleanly map to the protagonist's four domains (combat / labour-trades / offices / recognition) without over-splitting into a 4X spreadsheet; the kanji-flavour / English-UI split matches the discipline the PRD already applies to titles.

decisions_to_lock.question: Tier-gate combination rule: four-minimum floor + capped overflow, or a weighted sum / four hard ANDs?
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Four-minimum floor + capped overflow-substitution (≤25%), with per-tier weighting drift (Estate+Arms early → Office+Name late). Start strict for the grounded/cozy tone; expose both dials as data.
decisions_to_lock.why: A pure weighted sum lets one giant pillar carry the rest (re-enabling a pure-trade run — the exact failure the human rejected); four hard ANDs force tedious perfectly-even play. The floor+overflow rewards specialists without letting them skip a pillar — it IS the multi-pillar mandate made structural.

decisions_to_lock.question: Tier numbering: adopt the prompt's T0-T4 (Estate=T0, Edo=T4) or keep the PRD's T1-T5 (Estate=T1, Edo=T5)?
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Adopt the prompt's T0-T4 as canonical and add a one-line crosswalk note in PRD §1.6 (T0=old T1 Estate … T4=old T5 Edo).
decisions_to_lock.why: They are the SAME five tiers offset by one; the redesign and the existing §1.6/combat tables will read as contradictory unless reconciled in writing. Low-stakes but must be resolved when this graduates.

decisions_to_lock.question: Second rival house for the T3 takeover: add House Okabe (hereditary-conservative) beside the canon House Tomita (modern/commercial)?
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Add HOUSE OKABE as the old hereditary-conservative house holding the senior karo seat by birth; Tomita is out-COMPETED (substance vs bought advantage), Okabe is out-PERFORMED (merit vs inherited entitlement).
decisions_to_lock.why: Two archetypes let the effort>talent thesis land twice and give the takeover concrete, differently-flavoured targets. Keeps the cast lean (two well-drawn rivals, not 2+ vague ones) per the lean guardrail.

decisions_to_lock.question: Banzuke scope & form: one overall ranking, four separate column-charts, or an east-west banzuke with four-pillar sub-columns; and official vs mitate?
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: An east-west mitate-banzuke with four-pillar sub-columns plus an overall standing; explicitly a popular/parody (mitate) broadsheet, never an official bakufu register. Offer the 'banzuke of swordsmen' as the Arms pillar's optional public sub-chart.
decisions_to_lock.why: Four sub-columns + a roll-up gives branch-victory clarity AND clean UX; the mitate framing is the most D-010-safe and most historically authentic (the genre ranked anything). The mitate framing is load-bearing for the ceiling and must be confirmed at the §4/§5 research-harden.

decisions_to_lock.question: Castle-town takeover completion condition: top the domain banzuke on all four pillars, a majority, or any two?
decisions_to_lock.recommendation: Hold the karo seat OR a majority/plurality of the key offices, AND a daimyo-confidence lead over the leading rival, AND rivals subordinated — fired as 'top the DOMAIN banzuke'. Player can win T3 on any 2-3 of the five routes; unused routes become long-tail completionist goals.
decisions_to_lock.why: Keeps multiple viable paths (steward / banker / shield domains) so no single pillar is forced, satisfying 'grow in parallel' and giving replay without a reset.

open_questions: Exact per-pillar threshold VALUES and the seasonal 'judged result' FORMULA (how much each deed/milestone is worth per pillar; how the periodic result is computed from accumulated state) — defer to the §4 balance facet, but the SHAPE (floor + ≤25% overflow, min/geometric-mean roll-up, Estate ≤1/4 weight, trade ≤1/3 of Estate) needs sign-off.

Do the great real houses (Maeda/Shimazu/Date) appear by real name as banzuke 'yokozuna' flavour — adding grounding — or get fictionalised to honour the §1.13 'no real place-names' guardrail? (Real names collide with that guardrail; recommend fictionalised analogues.)

Where does the canon Daikan / Tedai-Kuroiwa officialdom cluster (PRD T4) sit relative to the new rival-house offices — is Kuroiwa a Tomita-aligned office-holder the Kurosawa displace, unifying the racket thread with the takeover?

Is the assertive/martial SECURITY route a FULL equal path to a top domain office, or leverage that accelerates the other routes? (Determines how genuinely first-class combat is at domain scale — it must give martial a non-optional route to a top office or combat reads decorative.)

The osso petition's 'whose neck' (protagonist / loyal ally / gimin-style martyr) and its timing (partial T3 redress, untouchable Edo apex glimpsed at T4) — reconcile with both partial-justice and the indirect ceiling so the risk doesn't read as personal heroic ennoblement.

Long-tail banzuke RE-RANK cadence and stakes: tie to the sankin-kotai biennial heartbeat (recommended), how hard rivals push, and confirm slipping is always cheaply recoverable (never a wipe, never a decay-tax that forces grind to stay put).

Which product is the canonical meibutsu (still OPEN in canon) — it gates the Estate-pillar trade capstone, the Nihonbashi category, and the banzuke product strand; lock before authoring the T4 economic/prestige payoff nodes.

Pacing lock: do the three side-threads (lost-child, origin-father, racket) all CLIMAX at the T3-T4 finale as pillar fuel (recommended), or resolve at T3 with T4 as a quieter restoration victory lap? (Open per §1.14.)

Time-budget split across the four pillars in the endgame so peaceful labour/trades/skills stay co-dominant with martial (target e.g. combat ≤~40% of endgame activity), honouring the locked 'peaceful labour is the dominant texture' pillar.

Confirm this whole multi-pillar endgame is explicitly a T3-T4 ROADMAP capstone (post-v1), not v1 scope (v1 = T0-T1 full, T2 stubbed), so it doesn't trip the lean-v1 / don't-over-build guardrails — and that the four-bar standing panel is the only piece of it that must exist in v1.

violations.constraint: Trade stays ONE pillar, never dominant (note 1) — applied to the ENDGAME, not just the macro.
violations.problem: The macro rebalance de-trade-weights well (trade capped to ~1/3 of one of four pillars, min/geometric-mean roll-up, enforced in generated tables — genuinely strong). But the ENDGAME quietly re-installs trade dominance the prompt explicitly told you to remove. (a) endgame_goals_and_set_pieces ACT 3 'THE MEIBUTSU & THE PURSE' literally preserves 'the graded meibutsu reaches its top category-rank in Nihonbashi' — the OLD §1.6/§1.11 T5 climax the human said is 'far too farmer/trade'. (b) The Estate per-pillar capstone is 'the meibutsu graded #1 in its category' — i.e. the single most iconic Estate mountaintop is STILL the trade sub-engine's optional capstone, not LAND or TREASURY. (c) The origin-father redemption is 'the origin caravan becomes the supply chain that carries the meibutsu to Edo' — the emotional family payoff is routed through trade. Across the four ACTs, trade gets a full headline ACT while LAND (shinden) and TREASURY each get nothing comparable; the meibutsu thus reads as ~1 of 4 endgame acts, not ~1/3 of 1 of 4 pillars. The macro math and the authored endgame contradict each other.

violations.constraint: Every pillar achievement/milestone-driven; NOT a passive time-trickle, NOT a flat per-action increment (locked rule).
violations.problem: The 'periodic JUDGED RESULT ... appraised once per season from accumulated state' is a smuggled time-trickle for the ECONOMIC and ARMS pillars. If idle producers raise koku/harvest passively and the season then 'judges' that accumulated state into +Estate every cycle, the player gains pillar score for the mere PASSAGE of seasons over a passively-growing base — exactly a time-trickle in an achievement costume. The JSON asserts 'not a per-action drip' but never closes the loop that the UNDERLYING STATE being appraised must itself be achievement-gated (a NEW recorded high, a NEW plot, a NEW solvency threshold), not 'this season's harvest met quota' repeating every season. The Arms seasonal results 'held the quarter through the lean season' and 'a standing patrol kept' are explicitly REPEATABLE per-season awards for maintaining a status quo = a trickle. The locked rule is violated unless seasonal results fire only on a NEW threshold crossed, never on steady-state maintenance.

violations.constraint: Mediocre-start contract: no power fantasy; combat catharsis is 'never power'; first real fight humbling; growth only by labour-built conditioning.
violations.problem: The endgame leans into set-pieces that strain the cozy/no-power-fantasy tone PRD §1.3 locks ('The catharsis is never power'). ACT 1 'lead the men-at-arms ... and stand the climactic siege/raid-defence of the estate' plus long-tail 'OPTIONAL GROUNDED SUPER-BOSSES ... the famine-winter siege ... at max difficulty' push toward a martial power-fantasy climax. The Hanzaki duel 'finally won ... beatable on labour-built endurance' is correctly framed, but a SIEGE the protagonist leads and wins, plus escalating super-bosses dropping 'the game's best FOUND gear', drift from 'peaceful Edo labour is the dominant texture' toward an action-RPG endgame. Not a hard contract-break, but it is the seam where mediocre-start/cozy-texture is most at risk and the JSON does not defend it.

violations.constraint: No implausible Edo leap; a goshi house cannot become a daimyo / openly rebel; offices held in the house's name with protagonist as deputy (D-010 ceiling).
violations.problem: Two over-reaches survive the otherwise-careful authenticity work. (a) The ARMS pillar / 'SHIELD-DOMAIN' route makes 'the house's retainers + the protagonist's combat become the seat's de-facto security' and fields 'men-at-arms' / a 'garrison' at castle-town scale. A goshi house maintaining a standing armed force large enough to be a domain's de-facto security is itself bakufu-alarming (private armies are precisely what the bakuhan order suppressed); 'martial leverage as a FULL equal route to a top domain office' (your own open_questions #4) edges toward the rebellion line the design claims to avoid. (b) 'the daimyo elevates the Kurosawa OVER the hereditary house' (Okabe, holding the senior karo seat by birth) — a goshi house displacing a hereditary karo house from the apex domain seat is a very large status leap; the Yoshida-Toyo precedent supports a risen man DIRECTING the domain, but Toyo did not erase the hereditary houses' RANK, and was assassinated for trying — your design has the Kurosawa SURVIVE and SUPPLANT, the more implausible outcome the precedent specifically warns against.

violations.constraint: Lean / high-impact; no fluff/bloat; v1 stays T0-T1 (don't over-build).
violations.problem: Cast and mechanism bloat. (1) Naming collision: 'origin-father Kuranosuke' collides head-on with the CANON 'Matagi headman Kuranosuke' (brainstorms/2026-06-25-world-expansion-factions.md §3a.4) — two different characters with the same name; the JSON never notices. The same brainstorm also uses 'Father Kuranosuke' as the origin trade/logistics contributor, so there may now be two or three Kuranosukes — unresolved canon corruption. (2) The four-kanji pillar system (武威/家産/政威/家格), four banzuke columns, four ACTs, three named domain-victory shapes, six set-pieces, four long-tail loops, three side-thread payoffs, three D-010 options, two new rival houses — a large authored surface for what the JSON itself admits is a 'T3-T4 roadmap capstone, post-v1'. The lean-v1 guardrail (PRD §1.14: v1 = T1-2 complete, T3 stubbed) is honored in a single closing sentence while the document's CENTER OF MASS is the T3-T4 endgame, inverting the 'don't over-build' discipline.

weaknesses: D-010 STATUS BURIED, NOT BLOCKING: The JSON correctly notes D-010 'is NOT yet written into docs/history/decisions.md (the log ends at D-006)'. I confirmed this — decisions.md ends at D-006; D-007, the D-004 reversal, AND D-010 are all authorized-but-unwritten. More load-bearing than the JSON frames it: the ENTIRE Arms pillar depends on D-007, and current canon §1.2 Pillar 4 (prd.md line 93) LITERALLY reads combat is 'never a source of standing' — the exact opposite. This redesign is built on three unwritten ADRs that contradict live canon. Locking D-007 + D-004-reversal + D-010 should be a hard PREREQUISITE gate, not a decisions_to_lock line item.

NAOYUKI UNRECONCILED: Canon (prd.md §1.8) already has an inside-the-family rival heir, Kurosawa Naoyuki, arc 'rivalry -> grudging respect -> brotherhood', a named talent-foil. The JSON introduces 'NEW HOUSE OKABE' as a second external rival and discusses Tomita at length but NEVER mentions Naoyuki. Adding Okabe while leaving Naoyuki unexamined risks rival-cast bloat and thematic muddle; the world-expansion brainstorm even re-casts Naoyuki as 'an ALLY against Tomita' — the JSON should have built on that, not silently added a third rival house.

SEASONAL JUDGED-RESULT FORMULA IS THE WHOLE GAME, DEFERRED: The single most important anti-trade-dominance and anti-trickle mechanism — four-minimum floor + <=25% overflow + min/geometric-mean roll-up + Estate<=1/4 + trade<=1/3-of-Estate — is asserted as prose; the threshold VALUES and seasonal-result FORMULA are deferred to '§4 balance'. The design's correctness LITERALLY depends on numbers nobody has written. If Estate is the easiest pillar to grow (three sub-engines + the idle economy underneath it), a '1/3 of Estate' trade lever can still be the path of least resistance. The skeptical read: de-trade-weighting is currently a PROMISE about future tuning, not a structural guarantee.

BANZUKE 'NO RESET' LONG-TAIL HAS A HIDDEN DECAY-TAX RISK: Long-tail loop (1) 'DEFEND THE TOP SPOT' re-ranks every sankin-kotai cycle and 'let a pillar go cold and a rival climbs above you'. The JSON insists this is 'never a decay-tax that forces grind merely to stay put' — but a re-ranking board where rivals 'push back' and you can be overtaken IS a soft decay-tax by another name; the distinction is entirely a tuning question left unresolved. With NO reset (D-004 reversed), a long-tail that punishes neglect is the classic incremental trap the locked direction was avoiding.

MITATE-BANZUKE FRAMING IS LOAD-BEARING BUT UNVERIFIED: The whole D-010 reconciliation rests on the banzuke being a 'popular/parody (mitate) broadsheet ... NOT an official bakufu register'. Historically defensible (chojya-banzuke existed) but the JSON flags it needs '§4/§5 research-harden' — so the single mechanism holding the honest ceiling intact is itself unconfirmed. If a 'banzuke of provincial houses' reads as quasi-official to players, the ceiling leaks. Also '政威 sei-i' is self-flagged as coined; coining kanji compounds for a design that prides itself on Edo authenticity is a small but real authenticity debt.

REAL DAIMYO NAMES vs NO-REAL-PLACE-NAMES GUARDRAIL: The endgame proposes Maeda of Kaga / Shimazu of Satsuma / Date of Sendai as the banzuke's sealed 'yokozuna' wallpaper. This collides with the LOCKED guardrail (PRD §1.13: 'Keep the world generic-rural — no real place-names', which already forced renaming a real shukuba to invented Sawatari-juku). The JSON flags it in open_questions and recommends fictionalizing — good — but presents it as an open choice when the guardrail has ALREADY been locked against exactly this, so the answer is forced (fictionalize), not open.

OSSO PETITION 'WHOSE NECK' STRAINS PARTIAL-JUSTICE + CEILING SIMULTANEOUSLY: The OFFICE capstone puts 'real personal risk on a chosen neck — protagonist / loyal ally / gimin-style martyr'. Putting the PROTAGONIST'S neck on the osso line as a heroic martyr-risk pulls against BOTH the indirect/mediated ceiling (he becomes the personally-heroic figure, not the back-of-the-room architect) AND risks reading as ennoblement-by-sacrifice. The JSON flags this in open_questions but it deserves to be near-violation: the safest read (gimin martyr is an ALLY; protagonist stays architect) should be the recommendation, not an open menu.
fixes: DE-TRADE-WEIGHT THE ENDGAME, NOT JUST THE MACRO. Rewrite ACT 3 so the Estate ACT headlines LAND or TREASURY, not the meibutsu — e.g. ACT 3 = 'THE DOMAIN'S DEBT RESCUED' (Treasury: the goyokin/debt-rescue that makes the daimyo's finance depend on the house), with the meibutsu demoted to one OPTIONAL beat inside it. Move the meibutsu out of the four-act spine entirely and make it ONE optional per-pillar capstone among three Estate capstones (a maxed shinden reclamation, a domain-creditor treasury, OR the graded meibutsu — player picks). Re-route the origin-father payoff so it is NOT 'the supply chain that carries the meibutsu to Edo' — Kuranosuke's debt bought back with resources from ANY pillar, decoupled from trade.

PIN THE SEASONAL JUDGED-RESULT RULE TO NEW-THRESHOLD-ONLY. State explicitly: a seasonal/audit result fires +pillar ONLY when the appraised state crosses a NEW high-water mark (a new recorded plot, a new solvency band, a new secured road) — never as a recurring award for maintaining steady state. Kill the repeatable maintenance awards ('held the quarter through the lean season', 'a standing patrol kept') as pillar SOURCES; make them flavour / dent-prevention only. This is the only way the locked 'not a time-trickle' rule survives an idle economy underneath.

MAKE LOCKING D-007 + D-004-REVERSAL + D-010 A HARD PREREQUISITE GATE. Promote it from a decisions_to_lock line to an explicit blocker: this redesign cannot graduate to canon until D-007 (combat earns standing — contradicts live prd.md §1.2 Pillar 4 line 93), the D-004 reversal, and D-010 are written as ADRs. Until then the Arms pillar contradicts canon and the multi-pillar model is non-canonical. Recommend D-010 OPTION A unchanged (it is the right call), but state the wall it depends on (mitate-not-official) must clear the §4/§5 research-harden in the same lock.

RESOLVE THE KURANOSUKE COLLISION AND RECONCILE NAOYUKI. (a) Rename the origin-father — canon already has 'Matagi headman Kuranosuke' AND 'Father Kuranosuke' as the origin trade/logistics contributor; pick ONE Kuranosuke or rename the origin father to a distinct name, and state which. (b) Address Naoyuki explicitly: confirm the canon rival-heir's 'rivalry -> brotherhood' arc and his world-expansion re-cast as 'ally against Tomita' still hold, and position House Okabe so it does not duplicate or eclipse Naoyuki. Cap the rival cast at Tomita + Okabe + (internal) Naoyuki with distinct functions, or drop Okabe if Naoyuki + Tomita already deliver the two effort>talent archetypes.

HARD-CAP MARTIAL SCALE TO STAY UNDER THE REBELLION LINE. State the house's armed force as a SMALL named retinue + temporary corvee/levies for crises, NOT a standing garrison/private army; frame 'de-facto security' as the house being the one the daimyo DEPUTISES for order (sanctioned, revocable), never an independent force. Make the assertive/security route lead to ONE security-flavoured office (machi-bugyo-adjacent) that still requires the daimyo's grant — not a route that could read as the house out-arming the seat. Resolve open_question #4: martial is a full route to a security office, but every martial gain is mediated by sanction, never raw force.

SOFTEN 'OVER THE HEREDITARY HOUSE' TO STAY EDO-PLAUSIBLE. Reframe the Okabe outcome so the Kurosawa come to DIRECT the domain (hold the working offices, win the daimyo's confidence) WITHOUT formally displacing Okabe's hereditary RANK — Okabe keeps the title, the Kurosawa hold the power (the authentic Toyo outcome). 'Eclipse', not 'replace at the apex seat'. Dramatize the Toyo-assassination warning: the Kurosawa survive BECAUSE they never reach for the formal apex rank, only the function — making the honest ceiling the reason they live, not a footnote.

FORCE THE FICTIONALIZE CALL AND DROP REAL DAIMYO NAMES. The no-real-place-names guardrail is already LOCKED; do not present real-daimyo banzuke-wallpaper as an open question — state the sealed top ranks are fictionalized great-house analogues, matching the Sawatari-juku precedent. Resolve the coined '政威 sei-i' by adopting an attested alternative (領 ryo / territorial sway) or a plainer attested compound at the same harden.

RE-SCOPE THE DOCUMENT AROUND v1. Lead with the ONE thing v1 needs (the four-bar standing panel + the four-pillar accrual rule + the floor/overflow gate shape) and explicitly bracket EVERYTHING else (T3 takeover, T4 banzuke, four ACTs, super-bosses, long-tail loops) as post-v1 roadmap not to be authored until T0-T1 ship. Move the seasonal-result FORMULA and threshold VALUES from 'deferred' to 'must be signed off before any tier-gate is tunable', since the whole de-trade-weighting claim depends on them.
verify.scope_assessment: SCOPE IS INVERTED RELATIVE TO THE LOCKED v1. Canon (prd.md §1.14) locks v1 = Tiers 1-2 complete, T3 stubbed, T4-5 roadmap. This redesign is almost entirely a T3 (castle-town takeover) + T4 (national banzuke) document — its center of mass is the post-v1 endgame. The JSON acknowledges this in its FINAL open_question ('confirm this whole multi-pillar endgame is explicitly a T3-T4 ROADMAP capstone, not v1 scope ... the four-bar standing panel is the only piece that must exist in v1') — the correct framing, but it arrives as the last of ten open questions rather than as the governing frame. The genuinely v1-relevant, structural contribution is small and sound: (a) House Influence becomes a four-pillar composite, (b) the four-minimum-floor + <=25%-overflow + min/geometric-mean roll-up gate, (c) the per-pillar accrual rule, (d) the four-bar standing panel — these are the load-bearing de-trade-weighting and belong in v1 thinking. The other ~80% (takeover routes, banzuke, ACTs, super-bosses, long-tail, two rival houses, six set-pieces) is roadmap fiction that should be explicitly fenced off so it does not trip the lean-v1 / don't-over-build guardrail. As written it is a strong DIRECTION doc but a scope-inverted PLAN.
verify.verdict: DOES NOT PASS as-is — but it is close, and the core multi-pillar idea is the right answer to the human's brief. The macro de-trade-weighting (trade -> 1/3 of 1 of 4 pillars, floor+overflow gate, min/geometric-mean roll-up) is genuinely strong and structurally forbids the pure-trade run the human rejected. No magic is introduced; mediocre-start is mostly honored; the D-010 reconciliation (Option A) is the correct call and the mitate-banzuke / lord-as-proxy framing keeps the honest ceiling intact at the HOUSE level. BUT five real violations block canonization: (1) trade dominance is removed from the macro yet quietly re-installed in the ENDGAME (the meibutsu still owns an entire finale ACT and the iconic Estate capstone — exactly what the human said to kill); (2) the 'seasonal judged result' is a smuggled time-trickle unless pinned to new-threshold-only, breaking the locked accrual rule; (3) two Edo-plausibility over-reaches (a goshi standing-army-as-de-facto-security, and displacing the hereditary karo house 'over' it) strain the no-rebellion / no-implausible-leap wall and may need an explicit D-010 re-lock rather than Option A's clean preservation; (4) cast/canon corruption — 'Kuranosuke' collides with a canon NPC and Naoyuki (the canon rival heir) is never reconciled against the new House Okabe; (5) scope is inverted — the doc is a T3-T4 endgame when v1 is locked at T0-T1. Additionally, this design sits atop THREE unwritten ADRs (D-007, D-004-reversal, D-010) that contradict live canon — most acutely prd.md line 93's 'combat ... never a source of standing', the literal opposite of the entire Arms pillar. Fix the endgame trade-weighting, pin the accrual rule, cap martial scale, resolve the name collisions, and re-frame around v1 + the prerequisite ADR lock, and this becomes lockable.

facets.facet: FACET A — The multi-pillar House-Standing model (macro rebalance of House Influence 家威)
facets.summary: I redesigned House Influence from the PRD §1.6 single trade-leaning score (7 "conversion levers" of which trade/meibutsu was the dominant endgame substance) into FOUR parallel achievement-driven pillars the player grows in lockstep: MARTIAL (武威 bu-i), ECONOMIC (家産 kasan), POLITICAL/TERRITORIAL (政威 sei-i), and PRESTIGE/CULTURAL (家格 kakaku). Trade is demoted to ONE of three sub-engines INSIDE the Economic pillar (the famous-meibutsu, which was the old climax, is now one capstone option in one pillar, not the recognition itself). Each pillar accrues only on concrete ACHIEVEMENTS + periodic seasonal MILESTONES (no trickle, no per-action increment) — every "+influence" event names a diegetic deed ("the road is declared safe and the books say so"; "the autumn audit records the surplus"; "the daikan posts your appointment"; "the chart names the house"). The tier climb (T0→T4) is gated by PER-PILLAR THRESHOLDS with a balanced-development floor (you must grow ALL FOUR past a minimum to advance, but can lead on any one or two) rather than a single weighted sum — this structurally forbids a pure-trade or pure-combat run from skipping tiers. Each pillar maps directly onto the protagonist's domains: Martial←combat/weapon-skills/leadership of men-at-arms; Economic←labour/jobs/skills/trades/crafting; Political←jobs-as-offices/administration/quests; Prestige←the recognition layer that reflects all three plus deeds/lineage/patronage. The national T4 endgame becomes a multi-pillar mitate-banzuke (a parody-ranking chart, historically authentic) on which the HOUSE climbs across all four columns — celebrating overall strength, not the product. This is honest to D-010: the HOUSE is ranked and recognised; the protagonist stays its architect/right-hand, never personally ennobled. I flag exactly where the T3 takeover and T4 ranking strain D-010 and give the human two reconciliation options. Grounding via Edo authenticity: kakaku merit-promotion (karo-kaku/-nami), goshi local office-holding (shoya/nanushi), koku-based domain wealth, and the real mitate-banzuke (chojya-banzuke "ranking of the rich") convention all anchor the model. The old PRD framed tiers as Estate→Village→Region→Domain→Edo (T1–T5); the prompt frames them T0–T4 — I map both and use the prompt's T0–T4 as canonical while noting the renumbering as an open question.

key_designs.name: Four pillars replace the single Influence score (家威 stays as the COMPOSITE name)
key_designs.description: House Influence (家威 ka-i) is retained as the UMBRELLA term for the house's overall standing but is no longer a single number — it is now the visible roll-up of four tracked pillar scores. (1) MARTIAL — 武威 (bu-i), 'martial authority': retainers/men-at-arms fielded, victories & clears, road/estate security held, rival houses' forces bested. (2) ECONOMIC — 家産 (kasan), 'house estate/wealth': lands held & reclaimed (koku base), treasury/creditworthiness, AND trade (the meibutsu/supply-chain is ONE sub-engine here, not the whole pillar). (3) POLITICAL/TERRITORIAL — 政威 (sei-i), 'governing authority': villages→region→castle-town under the house's sway, domain offices/administration held, alliances sealed, rival houses politically eclipsed. (4) PRESTIGE/CULTURAL — 家格 (kakaku), 'house rank/standing': formal kakaku rank, reputation, patronage given/received, notable deeds, lineage restored, recognition recorded in official ink. Each is a separate, mostly-monotonic, achievement-fed score; together they ARE 家威. Diegetically the player reads a four-bar 'house standing' panel, not one bar.

key_designs.name: Trade is de-weighted to ONE sub-engine inside ONE pillar
key_designs.description: Directly answers redesign note #1. The old §1.6 made the famous domain product / meibutsu / supply chain the dominant Influence path and the literal endgame substance celebrated at Edo. Now: the meibutsu lives INSIDE the Economic pillar as one of THREE economic sub-engines — (a) LAND (shinden reclamation, koku, the goshi-advancement classic), (b) TREASURY/FINANCE (debt→solvency→creditworthiness, goyokin), (c) TRADE (routes, broker standing, the optional meibutsu). A pure-trade player maxes at most ~1/3 of one of four pillars and is HARD-STOPPED at every tier gate by the other three pillars' thresholds. The meibutsu-reaching-Edo, formerly the climax, is now ONE optional capstone achievement in ONE sub-engine of ONE pillar — substantial but never the recognition itself.

key_designs.name: Per-pillar accrual = achievements + seasonal milestones, never a trickle
key_designs.description: Honors the locked 'achievement/milestone-driven, not a passive time-trickle, not a flat per-action increment' rule for ALL pillars. Two accrual shapes only: (A) ACHIEVEMENT JUMPS — discrete recognised deeds ('the bandit nest is cleared and the road declared safe'→Martial; 'the first shinden plot's yield is recorded'→Economic; 'the headman cedes the granary's administration to the house'→Political; 'the touring inspector's report names the house'→Prestige). (B) PERIODIC RESULTS — at each season/audit/harvest tick, the period's OUTCOME is appraised once and posted as a milestone ('this week's harvest exceeded quota: +Economic'; 'the men-at-arms held the quarter through the lean season with no losses: +Martial'). Crucially the periodic award is a JUDGED RESULT of accumulated state, not a per-action drip — flooding a good or grinding kills still post nothing until the season's books acknowledge them. Per-event caps (carried from the existing Combat-Deeds ~0.1-level/kill discipline) prevent any single source from spiking a pillar.

key_designs.name: Per-pillar threshold tier gates with a balanced-development floor (not a weighted sum)
key_designs.description: Answers note #2's 'how they combine to gate the tiers'. Each tier T_n defines a FOUR-VALUE threshold {Martial≥m, Economic≥e, Political≥p, Prestige≥pr}. To advance you must clear ALL FOUR minimums — a 'balanced-development floor' that structurally forbids skipping a tier on a single pillar (no pure-trade or pure-combat fast-track). ABOVE the floor, leading on one or two pillars still helps: a small composite-overflow bonus lets surplus in a strong pillar partially (capped, e.g. ≤25%) substitute for a lagging one, so specialists feel rewarded WITHOUT being able to ignore a pillar entirely. This is deliberately stricter than a pure weighted sum (which would let one giant pillar carry the rest) and looser than four hard ANDs (which would force perfectly even play). Tunable per tier: early tiers weight Economic+Martial (survive, earn keep, get strong); upper tiers weight Political+Prestige (the takeover and the national ranking are won socially).

key_designs.name: Each pillar is wired to a distinct protagonist domain (combat AND jobs/skills/trades)
key_designs.description: Answers note #2's 'how it ties to the protagonist's combat/jobs/skills/trades' and keeps the SPINE (a mediocre man growing via grind across combat AND labour) load-bearing. MARTIAL is fed by the combat pillar already specced (Combat Deeds pool, weapon-skill lines, mob clears, loot/gear, the men-at-arms leadership rungs R6/R8/R10/R12) — combat now EARNS standing through this pillar, satisfying the locked 'combat is a first-class pillar that earns standing' (D-007). ECONOMIC is fed by labour/jobs/skills/trades (farming/koku, foraging/woodcutting/crafting skills, the trade/broker network). POLITICAL is fed by JOBS-AS-OFFICES and quests (running the granary administration, holding a bailiff/jito-dai post, arbitrating villages, the daikan-office appointments, alliance quests). PRESTIGE is fed by the recognition LAYER that reflects the other three plus authored deeds, patronage (shrine/temple, scholars), and the lineage/true-name beats. Every skill/job the protagonist grinds therefore visibly feeds a named pillar — 'numbers go up' always maps to 'the house's standing in X rises'.

key_designs.name: T2/T3 castle-town takeover = four multi-pillar routes, 'peacefully or otherwise' within Edo bounds
key_designs.description: Answers note #3 honestly. Under the bakufu a goshi house CANNOT militarily storm a castle-town, so 'take over' = becoming the DOMINANT/leading house — seizing the key domain offices & administration, eclipsing the 2+ rival samurai houses. Four routes, each leaning on a pillar, all valid and combinable: (1) POLITICAL/ADMINISTRATIVE — accumulate domain offices (kanjo-kata recording, the daikan's favour, a magistracy-adjacent appointment) until the house effectively runs the seat's administration. (2) ECONOMIC LEVERAGE — become the seat's indispensable creditor/supplier (restructure others' debts, control the rice-coupon settlement, the broker network) so the daimyo's finance depends on the house. (3) PRESTIGE/ALLIANCE — marriage/adoption alliance, the daimyo's favour, patronage networks, and politically DETHRONING rivals via the banzuke/society standing. (4) MARTIAL LEVERAGE (security, NOT rebellion) — the house's men-at-arms become the seat's de-facto security (road patrols, raid defence, suppressing the rival houses' enforcer arms like Hanzaki), making the house the one the domain relies on for order. 'Dethroning rivals' (House Tomita + a second rival) is a contested-meter conquest across pillars — out-supply, out-administer, out-ally, out-secure them. The takeover completes when the house tops the DOMAIN-level banzuke (a T3 precursor to the national one).

key_designs.name: T4 national endgame = a multi-pillar mitate-banzuke (banzuke-style ranking of houses)
key_designs.description: Answers note #4 and replaces 'the product is celebrated in Edo' as the climax. The endgame is a NATIONAL, Edo-level RANKING CHART — a mitate-banzuke (historically authentic parody-ranking, cf. the chojya-banzuke 'ranking of the rich') of provincial houses, with FOUR COLUMNS (Martial / Economic / Political / Prestige) plus an overall east-west standing. The house starts UNRANKED (a stinging absence, mirroring the existing 'the chart that omits you' beat), appears first in a low rank on its strongest column, then climbs each column toward the upper ranks. Substantial multi-pillar SET-PIECES draw on ALL the protagonist's domains: the touring-inspector survey (judges fields+ledgers+order+the meibutsu+the people's condition — every domain at once, already specced as the T5 capstone); a national meibutsu grading (Economic-trade); a domain-defence/security commendation that travels up (Martial); a documented merit-appointment flowing down through the lord (Political+Prestige); a celebrated patronage/cultural deed (Prestige). Reaching the top ranks across all four columns IS the win condition — meaty, climactic, and about overall strength, not one product.

key_designs.name: D-010 reconciliation: the HOUSE rises and is ranked; the protagonist stays the architect
key_designs.description: Answers note #5 explicitly. D-010's honest ceiling (the protagonist never personally becomes hatamoto / received by the shogun; no rags-to-daimyo) is PRESERVED by making the BANZUKE rank HOUSES, not men — the Kurosawa name climbs the chart; the protagonist is its named architect/right-hand whose service lifts the LORD'S standing (the existing 'lord-as-proxy / sankin-kotai' mediated mechanism does the lifting). Recognition arrives mediated (through the rusui correspondence, down through the lord) and is RECORDED about the house. WHERE THE AMBITION STRAINS D-010 (flagged for the human): (i) a goshi house 'dominating the castle-town' and 'topping a national chart' is a bigger HOUSE ascension than the original 'lord's standing nudged up + a modest meibutsu' — even house-level, this is more than D-010 envisaged. (ii) 'Dethroning rival samurai houses' implies real status displacement at the seat, which pushes the plausible ceiling. Two reconciliation options offered below.

ideas: EXAMPLE PILLAR MILESTONES PER TIER (illustrative, to tune in §4). T0 Estate: Martial — survive the humbling first fight, clear the first pest/animal nest, stand a held night-watch; Economic — first shinden plot's yield recorded, kura solvent, first season's quota met; Political — named on the household roster, granted the home-fields bailiff (jito-dai) duty; Prestige — the lord's explicit recognition, the house off the foreclosure list. T1 Village: Martial — a valley road declared safe, 2-3 men-at-arms fielded on a rota, the grain store defended; Economic — cash-crop/proto-industry online, the mon on village contracts, a tradeable surplus; Political — the headman's regard won, the house arbitrates a village dispute, first errand-authority beyond the gate; Prestige — a sponsored festival/shrine repair, the valley calls it 'the Kurosawa works'. T2 Region: Martial — a bandit nest cleared, the high-pass route secured, a standing patrol; Economic — branch holdings, the Kuzuhara embankment securing yield, broker/warehouse standing; Political — 2+ neighbouring valleys allied/under broker-anchor, a district standing roll-up; Prestige — the embankment recognised as distinguished service, a head-temple's vouch. T3 Castle-town: Martial — the house is the seat's de-facto road-security, a rival's enforcer arm broken; Economic — the seat's indispensable creditor/supplier, debt restructured into creditworthiness; Political — domain offices held (kanjo-kata recording, the daikan's favour), rivals eclipsed, the house tops the DOMAIN banzuke; Prestige — a marriage/adoption alliance, a kakaku merit-elevation (karo-kaku). T4 Edo/national: Martial — a domain-defence/security commendation travels up; Economic — the meibutsu graded and celebrated in Edo markets (the OLD climax, now one column); Political — a documented merit-appointment flows down through the lord; Prestige — the house named on the national mitate-banzuke and climbing each column.

TIER RENUMBERING: the existing PRD/brainstorms use T1 Estate → T5 Edo (five tiers); the prompt's locked foundation uses T0 Estate → T4 Edo (five tiers, zero-indexed). They are the SAME five tiers offset by one. Recommend adopting the prompt's T0–T4 as canonical and adding a one-line crosswalk note in the PRD so the §1.6 table and the combat brainstorm's tier tags don't read as contradictory. (Flagged as an open question.)

DIEGETIC PILLAR NAMES vs UI LABELS: keep authentic kanji as flavour (武威 bu-i / 家産 kasan / 政威 sei-i / 家格 kakaku) but label the player-facing panel in plain English ('Arms', 'Estate & Wealth', 'Standing & Office', 'Name & Honour') for legibility — same discipline the PRD already applies to titles (authentic term as in-fiction narration, legible term in UI). 政威 sei-i is a coined compound; if a more attested term is wanted, 'kokoku/territorial sway' or simply '領 ryo (domains held)' could substitute — flag for the §4/§5 authenticity-harden.

ROLL-UP DISPLAY: render the four pillars as a quadrant/radar or a four-bar 'house standing' panel that visibly fills unevenly — a specialist's chart looks lopsided, a balanced house looks square. This makes the 'grow all four' design legible at a glance and turns the banzuke endgame into 'square off the chart, then climb every column'. Ties into the UI-as-progression pillar: the four-bar panel itself is a reveal (unlocks when Influence first becomes visible, ~R7/bailiff).

ANTI-TEDIUM GUARD (carried from §1.13): four pillars risk four-spreadsheet 4X tedium. Keep each pillar's accrual STORY-FRAMED (every jump is a named deed in the event log) and keep the periodic milestone a single judged result per season, not four meters ticking. The side factions (village, origin, scholars, pilgrimage, artisans) remain MULTIPLIERS/FEEDERS into the pillars, not new pillars — so the macro stays four columns, never twelve.

BALANCE-FLOOR TUNING KNOB: the per-tier minimums + the capped overflow-substitution (≤25%) are the two dials that set how 'specialist-friendly' vs 'forced-balanced' the game feels. Recommend starting strict (real four-minimum floor, small overflow) for the cozy/grounded tone, and exposing both as data so the §4 balance pass and headless regression tests can tune time-to-tier under different play styles (pure-combat, pure-trade, balanced) against the locked '~halves-time-to-tier when side factions woven in' budget.
risks: D-010 STRAIN (must be reconciled before canon): 'dominate the castle-town' + 'top a national banzuke' is a larger HOUSE ascension than D-010's original 'lord's standing nudged + modest meibutsu', even kept house-level. Option A (recommended, keeps D-010 intact): the banzuke is explicitly a PROVINCIAL/regional-houses chart or a mitate (parody/unofficial) chart, NOT an official bakufu ranking; 'dethroning rivals' = eclipsing them in local/domain standing, not displacing them from bakufu-recognised rank; the climb tops out at 'the leading house of the domain, noted nationally as a notable provincial house' — astonishing for a goshi house, still short of hatamoto/daimyo. Option B (loosen D-010): allow a bigger house ascension (e.g. the lord's kokudaka formally raised, the house's kakaku officially elevated) with a correspondingly bigger personal role for the protagonist — more triumphant, less honest-ceiling. The human must pick before this graduates to an ADR.

COMBAT-AS-PILLAR RE-LOCK DEPENDENCY: the Martial pillar's entire premise (combat EARNS standing) rests on D-007, which the brainstorms flag as authorized-but-NOT-yet-written. If D-007 is never locked, the Martial pillar contradicts the still-live old Pillar-4 ('combat is the rare exception, never a source of standing'). This model REQUIRES D-007 (and the multi-pillar reframe itself) to be locked as a superseding ADR before it is canon.

FOUR-PILLAR TEDIUM / 4X DRIFT: §1.13 already warns against city-builder/4X tedium with ONE Influence score; four pillars quadruple the risk of the macro becoming four spreadsheets. Mitigation is designed in (story-framed jumps, single judged seasonal result, side factions stay multipliers not pillars) but the risk is real and must be re-checked at the §4 balance pass and in headless pacing tests.

SPECIALIST-PLAYER FRUSTRATION: the balanced-development FLOOR deliberately blocks a pure-combat or pure-trade run from skipping tiers. A player who loves only combat may feel gated by needing Economic/Political progress (and vice-versa). The capped overflow-substitution softens this but does not remove it. This is an intentional design tension (it IS the 'multi-pillar' mandate) but should be playtested for whether the floor feels like earned breadth or like a chore.

BALANCE BLOWUP ACROSS FOUR PILLARS × FIVE TIERS: four pillars each spanning five order-of-magnitude tiers multiplies the number-range/scope risk §1.13 already flagged. Thresholds, per-event caps, overflow weights, and seasonal-result formulas are all new tuning surface; without a tight v1 vertical slice (T0–T1 fully, T2 stubbed) this could explode in scope.

TIER NUMBERING COLLISION: the PRD/brainstorms (T1–T5) and the prompt (T0–T4) disagree by one. If not reconciled in writing, downstream §2/§4/§5 authoring and the existing §1.6/combat tables will read as contradictory. Low-stakes but must be resolved when this graduates.
open_questions: Reconcile D-010: keep it intact via Option A (provincial/mitate banzuke, eclipse-not-displace, ceiling at 'leading house of the domain, nationally noted'), or loosen it via Option B (formally raised kokudaka/kakaku, bigger personal arc)? This is the load-bearing call the human must make before canon.

Exact PILLAR SET and NAMES: are four pillars (Martial/Economic/Political-Territorial/Prestige) right, or should Political and Territorial split into five, or Economic's trade sub-engine be pulled out? And confirm the diegetic kanji (武威/家産/政威/家格 — 政威 is coined) vs plain UI labels.
