TIER-GATE COMBINATION RULE: confirm the 'four-minimum floor + capped overflow-substitution (≤25%)' over the alternatives (pure weighted sum / four hard ANDs / per-tier shifting weights). And set the per-tier weighting drift (Economic+Martial early → Political+Prestige late).

PER-PILLAR THRESHOLD VALUES and the seasonal-result formula: how much each deed/milestone is worth per pillar, and how the periodic 'judged result' is computed from accumulated state — defer to the §4 balance facet, but the SHAPE needs sign-off.
TIER RENUMBERING: adopt the prompt's T0–T4 as canonical and add a crosswalk note (recommended), or keep the PRD's T1–T5?

BANZUKE SCOPE & FORM: is the national endgame chart a single overall ranking, four separate column-charts, or an east-west banzuke with four-pillar sub-columns? And is it official, semi-official (domain-circulated), or a mitate/parody chart (the most D-010-safe and historically authentic)?

CASTLE-TOWN TAKEOVER COMPLETION CONDITION: does 'taking over' require topping the DOMAIN banzuke on all four pillars, on a majority, or on any two? And are the 2+ rival houses each beaten on a specific pillar (a rock-paper-scissors of rivals) or all contested across all four?

DOES ANY PILLAR EVER DENT? §1.6 allows small recoverable Influence dents (a scandal, a called debt). With four pillars, do dents hit one pillar (a lost battle dents Martial; a scandal dents Prestige) or the composite? Recommend per-pillar dents for legibility — confirm against the 'never a wipe' feel.

SIDE-FACTION → PILLAR MAPPING: confirm which side factions multiply which pillars (e.g. Scholars→Economic+Political efficiency; Pilgrimage→Prestige; Origin trade-ties→Economic-trade; Matagi→Martial-utility) so the '~halves time-to-tier' budget is distributed across pillars, not piled on one.

facets.facet: Facet B — The Castle-Town Takeover (T3): how the Kurosawa come to DOMINATE the castle-town / domain seat, "peacefully or otherwise"
facets.summary:
WHAT "TAKEOVER" AUTHENTICALLY MEANS. Under the bakufu a goshi house cannot militarily seize a castle-town — open force = rebellion = the house is destroyed (this is the hard plausibility wall). So T3's win is NOT conquest of the castle; it is becoming the DOMINANT / leading house OF the domain by capturing its key administrative OFFICES, winning the daimyo's confidence, and eclipsing the rival houses that currently run things. The castle-town is the board; the prizes are the chairs around the daimyo (karo / jodai-karo / the bugyo offices / gun-bugyo / kanjo-bugyo / machi-bugyo / daikan). History gives us the exact template: Yoshida Toyo of Tosa — a man of modest standing — rose through administrative posts (gun-bugyo -> karo) on merit plus the daimyo's favour, came to effectively RUN the domain, installed allies, and displaced the conservative hereditary faction. And O-Ie Sodo (御家騒動, "house strife") is the authentic name for exactly this: factional struggle inside a domain's leadership over offices and succession, arbitrated by the lord and ultimately watched by the bakufu. So Facet B is a House-vs-House office-and-favour war, fought across all four pillars, that ends with the Kurosawa holding the domain's commanding offices and the rival houses subordinated, allied, or ruined.

THE REDESIGN FIX THIS DELIVERS. This is the facet that most directly de-trade-weights the macro (brief §1): the takeover is fought on FOUR pillars in parallel — MARTIAL, ECONOMIC, POLITICAL/TERRITORIAL, PRESTIGE — and trade is only ONE input. Economic leverage (goyokin, debt-rescue of the daimyo, rice-finance over rivals) is potent but POLITICAL capture of offices is the actual win condition, and MARTIAL standing (the house's retainers, security service, defeating rivals' men in sanctioned ways) is real leverage, not decoration. The protagonist is the ARCHITECT / right-hand who wins these offices FOR the Kurosawa head (preserving D-010's mediated ceiling at every rung).

THE RIVAL POWER-STRUGGLE. Two-plus entrenched houses currently share the domain's offices and the daimyo's ear. RIVAL HOUSE TOMITA (already canon, T3->T5 status rival) is the modern, capital-and-connections house that prospered as the Kurosawa fell — they hold the economic/commercial offices and the daikan's favour. A SECOND house — propose HOUSE OKABE (or fold in an existing hereditary-karo line) — is the OLD, hereditary, conservative house: birth-proud, complacent, holds the senior karo seat by lineage not merit. Tomita is beaten by out-competing (substance vs bought advantage); Okabe is beaten by out-PERFORMING (merit vs inherited entitlement — the game's core thesis weaponised at domain scale). A house can be DETENTE'd, ALLIED (marriage/adoption), cleanly DEFEATED commercially/politically, or — only via the racket-evidence thread — exposed and disgraced. None is killed; "or otherwise" tops out at sanctioned force and ruinous leverage, never assassination by the player (the real Yoshida Toyo was assassinated BY extremists — we make the Kurosawa the reformer who SURVIVES by being clean, and the rivals the ones who over-reach).

HOW THE FOUR PILLARS CONTRIBUTE & COMBINE. Each office has a pillar-cost: you cannot be APPOINTED to it until the relevant pillar(s) clear a threshold AND a rival is displaced from it. Martial wins security offices; economic wins finance offices; political/prestige wins the senior advisory seats. The "leading house" win condition is a DOMAIN STANDING roll-up: hold N of the domain's key offices + the daimyo's confidence above the leading rival + rivals subordinated. Multiple viable routes exist because different office-sets satisfy the win — a martial-security house, a finance house, and an administrative-reformer house each reach "leading" by a different door.

NUMBERING NOTE: the redesign brief calls the castle-town T3 (T0 Estate); the existing PRD/factions docs call it T4 (T1 Estate, Edo=T5). This facet IS the brief's T3 = the docs' T4. I use the brief's numbering and cross-reference where it matters.

key_designs.name: What 'take over a castle-town' authentically MEANS (the plausibility wall, made into the design)
key_designs.description: HARD CANON: a goshi house cannot militarily storm or seize a castle-town — under the bakuhan order open force against the domain seat is rebellion, which means immediate bakufu intervention and the house's destruction (this is the bright line that keeps D-010 and grounded-no-magic intact). 'Takeover' is therefore redefined as becoming the DOMINANT / LEADING HOUSE of the domain: holding the commanding administrative OFFICES, owning the daimyo's confidence, and reducing the rival houses to subordinate / allied / ruined status. The castle stays the daimyo's; the Kurosawa come to RUN it for him. Historical anchors that license this exactly: (a) Yoshida Toyo of Tosa rose from gun-bugyo (district magistrate, ~1842) to karo (1853) on the daimyo Yamauchi Yodo's favour + reform competence and came to effectively direct the entire domain, installing allies and displacing the conservative hereditary faction — a modest-standing man running a domain by merit is REAL; (b) O-Ie Sodo (御家騒動, 'honourable house strife') is the period's own name for factional conflict inside a domain's leadership over offices and succession, taken seriously by the bakufu and dramatised in kabuki — the authentic frame for the whole facet; (c) the daimyo was frequently absent in Edo (sankin-kotai), so the jodai-karo and the bugyo offices in the castle-town WERE the de-facto government — capturing them IS capturing the domain. This single reframe converts the brief's tension ('honest about Edo authenticity') into the design's spine rather than a caveat.

key_designs.name: The prize: the domain's key OFFICES (the actual win condition of T3)
key_designs.description: The takeover is fought over a concrete office ladder physically seated in the castle-town. Authentic offices, each tagged to the pillar(s) that win it and the rival who currently holds it: (1) DAIKAN / gun-bugyo — district administration & tax (POLITICAL+ECONOMIC; the canon Daikan Iemasa + Tedai Kuroiwa sit here; this is the PRD's existing T4 recognition engine — the first real office the Kurosawa hold). (2) KANJO-KATA / kanjo-bugyo — domain finance & treasury (ECONOMIC; held by Tomita's faction; won by rescuing the domain's debt, not just the house's). (3) MACHI-BUGYO-sho — castle-town magistracy, law, security, the petition hall (MARTIAL+POLITICAL; security crises and the osso petition are decided here). (4) The senior KARO / jodai-karo seat — the daimyo's standing chief councillor who runs the domain in his absence (PRESTIGE+POLITICAL, the apex prize; held by the OLD hereditary house by birth — the Yoshida-Toyo target). (5) YONIN / sobayonin-style attendant-advisor — informal proximity to the daimyo's ear (PRESTIGE; the soft path to all the others). WIN CONDITION = a DOMAIN STANDING roll-up: the Kurosawa (via its head, with the protagonist as architect) hold a majority/plurality of these offices OR specifically the karo seat, the daimyo's confidence exceeds the leading rival's, and rivals are subordinated. Each office unlocks at a pillar threshold AND requires displacing/out-competing its current holder — so it is a contest, not a purchase. Note: office is APPOINTED to the Kurosawa HOUSE/head; the protagonist holds a deputy/right-hand post under it, never an office beyond a merit-risen retainer's plausibility (D-010 preserved per-office).

key_designs.name: The rival houses as a live power-struggle (O-Ie Sodo at goshi scale)
key_designs.description: Two+ entrenched houses currently share the offices and the daimyo's ear; the Kurosawa rise by taking those offices FROM them. HOUSE TOMITA (canon) = the MODERN rival: capital + connections + ruthlessness, prospered as the Kurosawa fell, holds the finance/commercial offices and the daikan's favour; beaten by OUT-COMPETING (substance vs bought/lucked/ruthless advantage — binds the talent-foil rule). SECOND house — propose HOUSE OKABE = the OLD hereditary-conservative house: birth-proud, complacent, holds the senior karo seat by LINEAGE not merit; beaten by OUT-PERFORMING (the game's effort>talent thesis at domain scale; the reformer-vs-hereditary axis Yoshida Toyo embodied). Each rival is a contested set of meters (office-holds, daimyo-favour, valleys/brokers/society standing) you flip pillar by pillar. RESOLUTIONS per rival (player-shaped, never killed by the player): DETENTE (a negotiated power-share), ALLIANCE (marriage/adoption tie — the rival's strength absorbed), CLEAN DEFEAT (out-competed/out-performed until the daimyo reassigns their office to the Kurosawa), or DISGRACE (only via the optional racket-evidence thread: a rival exposed for corruption and stripped of office by the daikan/metsuke — partial justice, honouring the existing 'apex escapes' rule). A squeezed valley flipping to the fairer Kurosawa, or a rival over-reaching into a security crisis or an osso scandal, shows ruthlessness has a cost. CRUCIAL HONESTY: the historical reformer (Toyo) was ASSASSINATED by the faction he beat — we INVERT this: the Kurosawa are the clean reformers who SURVIVE because they win by daylight, and a desperate rival's resort to violence/coercion is what discredits THEM (a security-crisis set-piece, below).

key_designs.name: The MULTIPLE PATHS (peaceful and assertive, all Edo-plausible)
key_designs.description: Several genuinely different routes reach 'leading house,' each a different office-set + pillar-mix, so replay/identity matters. PEACEFUL ROUTES: (A) ADMINISTRATIVE-APPOINTMENT / REFORMER (the Yoshida-Toyo path; primary POLITICAL): render distinguished service (flood-control = the Kuzuhara embankment, famine-relief, a clean audit), be appointed daikan/gun-bugyo -> kanjo-kata -> rise toward karo by reform competence + the daimyo's favour; install Kurosawa allies in vacated posts. (B) ECONOMIC LEVERAGE (ECONOMIC): rescue the DOMAIN's strangling debt via goyokin + honest rice-finance (the canon Marutaya restructuring scaled up to the daimyo's own treasury) until the domain literally cannot function without the Kurosawa — financial indispensability buys the finance office and the daimyo's gratitude (authentic: domains were chronically indebted; merchant-financiers were given samurai status and de-facto policy weight; goyokin was the lever). (C) MARRIAGE / ADOPTION ALLIANCE (PRESTIGE+POLITICAL): a sanctioned tie (the canon Omiya-no-Sahei nakodo; Osato of Marutaya; a domain-retainer line; or absorbing a rival house by adoption) leaps the house up a rung and can neutralise a rival by merger. (D) THE DAIMYO'S FAVOUR / proximity (PRESTIGE): the yonin/attendant path — win the daimyo's personal confidence (counsel, culture, hospitality via Lady Chiyo, the lord's lifted standing) so he ELEVATES the Kurosawa over the hereditary house. (E) POLITICAL OUT-MANEUVERING (POLITICAL): build a bloc — allied valleys (the Neighbouring Valleys cluster), peer retainers (Tadahiro), pious/scholarly patrons (Abbot Tetsugen, Academy-head Rai Genpachi) — until the Kurosawa command more of the domain's body than the rivals, an O-Ie-Sodo coalition the daimyo must side with. ASSERTIVE / 'OR OTHERWISE' ROUTES (within bakufu bounds — pressure, never rebellion): (F) MARTIAL / SECURITY LEVERAGE (MARTIAL): the house's retainers and the protagonist's combat strength become the domain's de-facto SECURITY arm — putting down a bandit menace (the racket's enforcer arm / Hanzaki), guarding the kaido, defeating a RIVAL's retainers in sanctioned bouts/disputes/a security crisis — making the Kurosawa the house the daimyo relies on for order. This is leverage and indispensability, NOT storming the castle. (G) COERCION / LEVERAGE within bounds (mixed): call in a rival's debts, expose their racket complicity (the evidence thread), exploit a CRISIS (famine, flood, a security panic, an inspection) where the rival fails and the Kurosawa deliver. Most playthroughs MIX routes; the four-pillar threshold model means no single route is forced, satisfying brief §2's 'grow in parallel.'

key_designs.name: Pillar-by-pillar accrual + how they combine into the takeover (the mechanics)
key_designs.description: Each pillar accrues by ACHIEVEMENT/MILESTONE (never time-trickle), and each gates different offices: MARTIAL accrues on retainers recruited/trained, security services rendered (bandit menace broken, kaido secured, a rival's retainers defeated in sanctioned dispute, a crisis quelled) -> gates machi-bugyo / security trust + is raw leverage on every rival. ECONOMIC accrues on the domain treasury rescued, goyokin posted, rice-finance and meibutsu volume the domain depends on, debt restructured -> gates kanjo-kata + makes the daimyo financially beholden. POLITICAL/TERRITORIAL accrues on offices held, valleys/region controlled (the Neighbouring Valleys bloc), alliances sealed, rivals subordinated -> gates daikan->karo + IS the win-condition substrate. PRESTIGE/CULTURAL accrues on the daimyo's favour, patronage (temple/academy vouches), notable deeds publicly recorded, marriage/adoption standing, lineage restored -> gates the yonin/karo seats + the daimyo's choice to elevate the Kurosawa over the hereditary house. COMBINATION: a DOMAIN STANDING composite (the T3 face of House Influence's multi-pillar rebalance) rolls the four up; an office is APPOINTED when its required pillar(s) clear threshold AND its current rival-holder is displaced; 'leading house' fires when the Kurosawa hold the office majority/the karo seat + daimyo-confidence lead. Because offices map to different pillars, a player heavy in one pillar still reaches 'leading' by a different office-door — multi-pillar by construction, trade de-weighted to one of four inputs.

key_designs.name: The takeover SET-PIECES (the dramatised climaxes)
key_designs.description: Several stagable set-pieces so the conquest FEELS real (not a bar filling): (1) THE OFFICE APPOINTMENT — the daimyo (or jodai-karo in his stead) formally names the Kurosawa to a domain office over the rivals' objection; staged in the castle audience hall; the first time the house sits where a rival sat. (2) THE OSSO / PETITION HALL SHOWDOWN — the machi-bugyo-sho case where the racket/rival is challenged on documented proof; a rival faction's man is stripped of office or the Kurosawa's service is vindicated (canon osso thread, scaled to inter-house). (3) THE SECURITY CRISIS — a bandit surge / a flood / a riot the rival house FAILS to handle and the Kurosawa's retainers + the protagonist's combat resolve, converting martial strength into the daimyo's trust and a security office (the assertive route's headline; a real fight beat, the rare dangerous combat the combat pillar earns). (4) THE GOYOKIN / DEBT-RESCUE — the domain's finances are failing; the Kurosawa post the loan no rival can, and the daimyo grants the finance office in gratitude (the economic route's headline). (5) THE ALLIANCE / O-IE-SODO RESOLUTION — the negotiated end of the house-struggle: a marriage seals a rival's absorption, OR the daimyo arbitrates and reassigns offices to the Kurosawa, OR a rival over-reaches (violence/coercion) and discredits itself; the bakufu's metsuke is the off-stage arbiter whose disapproval ANYONE risks by going too far (the rebellion guardrail dramatised). (6) THE METSUKE/INSPECTION as legitimacy test — the existing touring-inspector set-piece, here judging WHICH house best runs the domain (substance vs show; the inspector validates the Kurosawa's rise, bridging T3->T4/Edo). Each set-piece draws on a specific pillar so the climax 'draws on ALL the protagonist's domains' (brief §4).

key_designs.name: D-010 reconciliation at the T3 layer (flagged for the human)
key_designs.description: D-010 (human-confirmed; captured in PRD §1.6 T5 row) = Edo recognition is INDIRECT/MEDIATED; the protagonist never personally becomes hatamoto / received by the shogun; no rags-to-daimyo. T3's 'take over the castle-town' strains this LESS than the Edo endgame does, but it still needs an explicit ruling because 'the house runs the domain' is ambitious. The clean reconciliation, holding D-010 intact: the OFFICES are held by the Kurosawa HOUSE/HEAD (Lord Munenori / heir Naoyuki); the PROTAGONIST holds a deputy/right-hand/yonin post UNDER them as the architect — exactly a merit-risen retainer's plausible ceiling (Yoshida Toyo himself rose to karo, but he was already samurai; our protagonist stays one rung below the formal apex, the house's indispensable steward, not its lord). The daimyo's seat is NEVER taken — the Kurosawa lead the domain's RETAINER houses, they do not displace the daimyo. EXPLICIT FLAG / OPTIONS FOR THE HUMAN: (i) KEEP D-010 as-is — house holds offices, protagonist stays right-hand (recommended; fully plausible). (ii) LOOSEN slightly — allow the protagonist himself to be appointed to a real domain office (gun-bugyo/yonin) by merit, since the historical Toyo precedent supports a risen man holding office; this is a modest, defensible loosening that strengthens the personal arc without breaking the 'never hatamoto/never received by shogun' core. (iii) The harder question is the EDO endgame (Facet's siblings) — T3 can stand on (i) regardless of how Edo is ruled. Recommend the human rule (i) or (ii) before the T3 office-appointment set-pieces are authored, since it sets the protagonist's exact title at the top of T3.

ideas: Name the second rival HOUSE OKABE — the OLD hereditary-conservative house holding the senior karo seat by birth, complementing the existing modern/ruthless House Tomita. Two rival ARCHETYPES (bought-modern vs entitled-hereditary) let the effort>talent thesis land twice: out-COMPETE Tomita, out-PERFORM Okabe.

Model the offices as a literal CHAIRS board in the castle audience hall UI: each domain office is a seat currently occupied by a rival's mon; the takeover is visibly the Kurosawa mon replacing rival mons seat by seat — a legible, fractal, de-weighted-from-trade Influence display.

Make the DAIMYO'S CONFIDENCE its own prestige meter contested head-to-head with each rival house (a relative bar, not absolute) — the single most authentic lever (Yoshida Toyo rose entirely on Yamauchi Yodo's favour). Losing the daimyo (e.g. his Edo recall/disgrace) is a scripted recoverable DENT, mirroring the historical fragility of favour-based power.

Use the SECURITY-CRISIS set-piece as the combat pillar's domain-scale payoff: a bandit surge or riot the rival house fumbles and the Kurosawa retainers + protagonist quell — the rare dangerous fight that EARNS a security office, making combat first-class in the takeover (not just economy/politics).

Let a defeated rival become an ASSET, not a corpse: detente = power-share, marriage/adoption = absorb their strength, clean defeat = inherit their offices, disgrace (evidence-only) = strip their offices. Four authentic, non-lethal resolutions per house keep 'or otherwise' within bakufu bounds.

Tie the GOYOKIN lever explicitly to the DOMAIN treasury (not just the house's debt): rescuing the daimyo's OWN finances is the economic route's killer move and is historically exact (chronically indebted domains, merchant-financiers handed samurai status and policy weight).

Reserve VIOLENCE/COERCION beyond sanctioned bounds for the RIVALS, not the player: a desperate rival who resorts to coercion, a rigged crisis, or (echoing the real Toyo assassination) tries to remove the Kurosawa, and thereby DISCREDITS itself before the metsuke — dramatising the rebellion guardrail as the rivals' undoing.

Make the METSUKE/touring-inspector the off-stage arbiter of the whole O-Ie-Sodo: any house that goes too far (open force, fraud) risks bakufu attainder — the bright line is enforced diegetically, and the inspection set-piece judges which house best RUNS the domain, bridging T3 into T4/Edo.

Offer 3 named 'leading house' victory shapes for flavour/replay: THE STEWARD-DOMAIN (won on administrative reform + offices), THE BANKER-DOMAIN (won on finance indispensability), THE SHIELD-DOMAIN (won on security/martial trust) — each a different office-set satisfying the same win condition.

Seed the rival power-struggle EARLY and fractally per the existing reveal cadence: Tomita first met T3 as 'a single name quietly underbidding a deal'; Okabe first met as 'the proud old house that won't return the Kurosawa head's bow' — one contact, one slight, then the contest blooms.
risks: NUMBERING COLLISION: the brief calls the castle-town T3; the existing PRD/factions docs call it T4 (Estate=T1, Edo=T5). If not reconciled in canon, 'T3' is ambiguous across documents. The whole macro-rebalance should pick ONE tier scheme before this facet is written into the PRD; this facet uses the brief's T0-T4.

D-010 strain: 'the house runs the domain' is more ambitious than the locked mediated ceiling. Mitigated by holding offices at HOUSE/head level with the protagonist as right-hand, but the human must explicitly rule (keep / loosen) — flagged in key_designs. Authoring office-appointment set-pieces before that ruling risks rework.

Authenticity over-reach if not policed: any hint of the player militarily SEIZING the castle, deposing the daimyo, or rags-to-daimyo breaks both plausibility and D-010/D-003. The 'leading house, never the lord; offices not the castle; pressure not rebellion' line must be a guardrail, not a footnote.

4X / strategy-game drift: an office-war with rival-house meters across four pillars risks becoming a domain-management spreadsheet, violating the lean/high-impact + 'must not drift into city-builder/4X tedium' guards. Each office/pillar must stay a small, diegetic, recognition-gated milestone loop, not a sim.

Pillar-balance / dominance: if any one pillar (esp. economic) clears the most offices, the brief's de-trade-weighting fails again. The office->pillar mapping must be tuned so martial and political each independently command apex offices, and 'leading house' is reachable without economic supremacy.

Combat-pillar tokenism: combat is a FIRST-CLASS pillar but the takeover is mostly political/economic. The security-crisis set-piece(s) must give martial strength a genuine, non-optional ROUTE to a top office, or combat reads as decorative at domain scale.

Two-plus rival houses + multiple paths + set-pieces is a lot of content; risks scope blow-up against lean/high-impact. May need a THIN spine (one office ladder, one primary rival to displace) with the second rival and assertive routes as optional accelerants, mirroring the existing spine-vs-side discipline.

Historical precedent (Yoshida Toyo) ends in ASSASSINATION of the reformer — if mishandled the tone could turn grimdark or imply the player should assassinate. The design inverts it (rivals over-reach, player stays clean) but writers must hold that inversion or the no-magic/grounded-but-hopeful tone slips.
open_questions: Tier numbering: does canon adopt the brief's T0-T4 (castle-town=T3) or keep the docs' T1-T5 (castle-town=T4)? Needs a single ruling before this facet enters the PRD.

D-010 ruling for T3: keep the protagonist as house right-hand under house-held offices (recommended), OR loosen to let the protagonist personally hold a domain office (gun-bugyo/yonin) by merit per the Yoshida Toyo precedent? This sets his exact title at the top of T3.
How many rival houses — exactly two (Tomita modern + a new Okabe hereditary), or 2+ with minor houses as office-holders to flip? Lean argues for two well-drawn archetypes.

What is the precise 'leading house' WIN CONDITION numerically — hold the karo seat specifically, OR a majority of N offices, OR a daimyo-confidence lead over all rivals? (Affects whether the apex prize is one office or a portfolio.)
Is the assertive/martial SECURITY route a full equal path to a top office, or leverage that accelerates the other routes? (Determines how first-class combat really is at domain scale.)

Should a rival house ever be DISGRACED/destroyed (via the racket-evidence thread stripping its offices), or only ever subordinated/allied? (Tests the 'partial justice / apex escapes' rule at house scale.)

Does the daimyo's confidence carry a real DENT risk (his Edo recall/disgrace/death) as a scripted setback, mirroring the historical fragility of favour-based power — and is that fun or just punishing?

Where does the existing canon Daikan/Tedai-Kuroiwa officialdom cluster (PRD T4) sit relative to these new rival-house offices — is Kuroiwa a Tomita-aligned office-holder the Kurosawa displace, unifying the racket thread with the takeover?

How does T3's 'leading house of the domain' hand off into the Edo/national banzuke endgame (sibling facet) — is being 'leading house' the entry ticket to the national ranking, and does that handoff need its own gate?

facets.facet: FACET C — The National Edo Endgame: a multi-pillar banzuke ranking (T4 climax)
facets.summary: Replace the current T4 climax ("the renowned domain *meibutsu* celebrated in Edo's markets" — the trade-weighting the human wants gone) with a **national, mitate-banzuke-style RANKING of HOUSES** scored across four pillars (MARTIAL / ECONOMIC / POLITICAL-TERRITORIAL / PRESTIGE-CULTURAL). The trade/meibutsu becomes ONE input to ONE pillar (Economic), not the win condition. The Edo endgame is the Kurosawa climbing this published banzuke of provincial houses — passing the rival **Tomita** for the domain's top slot, then rising the regional/provincial chart toward (but never into) the rarefied tier of the great real houses (**Maeda/Kaga, Shimazu/Satsuma, Date/Sendai** as untouchable "yokozuna" flavour at the chart's head). This is grounded: the mitate-banzuke (parody ranking charts of rich men, restaurants, even disasters) was a real, beloved Edo print genre, so a "banzuke of provincial houses" reads as an authentic woodblock broadsheet, not a game-y leaderboard. The win state is the HOUSE entering the chart's named upper ranks and being publicly reckoned a first-rank provincial house — recognition that **celebrates overall influence & strength**, drawing set-pieces from ALL the protagonist's domains (combat, jobs, skills, trades, leadership). D-010 (indirect/mediated Edo ceiling) is KEPT: the protagonist is never personally on the chart, never hatamoto, never received by the shogun — the *house name* is ranked and the protagonist is its uncredited architect (realistically he tops out at **karō-kaku**, house-elder-equivalent chief retainer, the highest a merit-risen non-born man plausibly reaches). One honest strain on D-010 is flagged for the human, with three reconciliation options.

key_designs.name: The Banzuke as the macro-resource's public face (家威 made visible)
key_designs.description: House Influence (家威) currently a single hidden number now surfaces at T4+ as a literal woodblock-printed mitate-banzuke of houses — the authentic Edo parody-ranking format (top-center title/date, EAST and WEST columns, ranks descending Yokozuna→Ōzeki→Sekiwake→Komusubi→Maegashira-1..N with calligraphy shrinking down the chart, sponsor/compiler names in the margins). The Kurosawa start as an unranked also-ran printed in the tiny bottom-margin 'mae-zumo' names; climbing = your name printed larger, higher, and finally promoted East/West rank. Crucially this is NOT one combined number: the published chart is composed from FOUR pillar sub-rankings (see below), so the player reads four bars climbing in parallel and a roll-up rank. This makes 家威 diegetic ('the new banzuke is out — the Kurosawa have moved up two places on the martial side') and is the single most legible Influence display of the whole game, replacing the old 'meibutsu's chart-rank' as the visible win condition but de-coupling it from trade.

key_designs.name: FOUR scored pillars, each achievement/milestone-accrued (de-trade-weighted)
key_designs.description: MARTIAL (武): retainers/men-at-arms recruited & equipped, the protagonist's own combat record (named-mob/duel victories, weapon-skill mastery), security crises resolved (bandits, the pass, river-disaster defence), rival retainers bested, the house's drill-yard reputation — scored on standing forces + decisive deeds, NOT per-kill trickle. ECONOMIC (財): lands reclaimed (koku), treasury/creditworthiness, AND trade/meibutsu — but trade is capped as ~1/3 of this ONE pillar, so the famous product matters yet can never dominate the chart. POLITICAL/TERRITORIAL (政): villages→valleys→region→castle-town under Kurosawa sway, domain OFFICES & administration held (jitō-dai→kanjō-kata seat→a domain post), alliances/marriages sealed, rival houses politically eclipsed (Tomita's offices & favour stripped). PRESTIGE/CULTURAL (名): reputation/notable deeds, patronage (a vouched scholar, a head-temple's blessing, a famous swordsman trained at your yard), lineage restored & surpassed, the inspector's favourable report, fame attaching to the house name. Each pillar accrues only on RECOGNIZED milestones (a sealed contract, a granted office, a won duel-of-houses, a printed gazetteer mention) — never a passive time-trickle, honouring the locked achievement-based Influence rule. The four combine into the published rank via a weighted roll-up that the player can see and steer (lean martial, lean political, etc.).

key_designs.name: The rival ladder: Tomita (peer), the domain's leading house (target), the great houses (untouchable ceiling)
key_designs.description: Three named strata on the chart. (1) RIVAL: House Tomita (existing) is the direct peer — a goshi/merchant house that prospered as the Kurosawa fell; on the banzuke they sit just above you for most of T4, and the central T4 contest is OVERTAKING Tomita across pillars (out-arming, out-administering, out-prestiging, flipping their allied valleys). (2) TARGET: the domain's current first-rank house (the daikan-favoured incumbent occupying the offices you want) — eclipsing them = becoming the domain's leading/dominant house, the castle-town takeover. (3) CEILING (flavour, never reachable): the chart's 'yokozuna' head is occupied by the real great houses as historical wallpaper — **Maeda of Kaga (~1.03M koku), Shimazu of Satsuma (~729k), Date of Sendai (~626k)** — a goshi house can rise to a respectable provincial Maegashira/Komusubi rank but the Ōzeki/Yokozuna slots are structurally sealed (the wall the truly powerful built — same partial-justice motif as the magistrate who escapes). This grounds the banzuke and makes D-010's honest ceiling visible AS the chart's geometry.

key_designs.name: The Castle-Town Takeover (T3→T4 pivot) feeding the political pillar — 'peacefully or otherwise'
key_designs.description: The political/territorial pillar is where the human's §1.11.3 castle-town takeover lives, with multiple routes that all feed the same rank: PEACEFUL — administrative appointment (climb daikan→tedai→a domain kanjō-kata office), economic leverage (the house becomes the domain's creditor via debt-restructuring/goyokin), marriage/adoption alliance (the Marutaya/Osato or a domain-retainer line), winning the daimyo's favour via the inspector's report, politically dethroning Tomita & the incumbent (stripping their offices and the daikan's favour). ASSERTIVE/FORCEFUL — martial pressure as LEVERAGE not rebellion: your retainers become the castle-town's de-facto security after a crisis the incumbent fluffs; defeating Tomita's retainers in sanctioned duels/contests; coercive economic squeeze. Honest Edo bound (per the human's own note): 'take over' = becoming the DOMINANT/leading house and seizing the key domain offices, NOT storming the castle — martial strength is leverage and security, never open bakufu-defying revolt. All routes raise the political (and bleed into martial/economic) pillar score; the takeover is 'complete' when the Kurosawa hold the leading house's offices and out-rank Tomita on the published domain banzuke.

key_designs.name: Edo milestone SET-PIECES drawing on ALL domains (the meaty climax)
key_designs.description: A ladder of climactic T4/T5 set-pieces, each pulling from a different protagonist domain so the endgame is multi-pillar, not a trade victory lap: (a) THE STINGING BROADSHEET — Konoe/the gesakusha forwards a mitate-banzuke on which Tomita is ranked and the Kurosawa absent (the call to climb; existing beat repurposed as the chart's intro). (b) THE DUEL OF HOUSES / drill-yard challenge — a sanctioned martial contest vs Tomita's retainers (uses combat pillar). (c) THE FAMINE/FLOOD CRISIS — relief-rice + flood-control + your men-at-arms keeping order while the incumbent flounders (economic+martial+political at once). (d) THE OFFICE GRANTED — the protagonist (as karō-kaku) is handed a domain administrative seat in the lord's name (political capstone; the lord, not the man, is honoured). (e) THE INSPECTOR'S REPORT travels to Edo (existing Junkenshi set-piece — the documented service that mints the prestige pillar's top step). (f) THE NEW BANZUKE READ ALOUD — the published chart names the Kurosawa in the upper ranks for the first time, eclipsing Tomita (the emotional payoff). The capstone is the HOUSE entering the chart's named ranks; the protagonist watches from the back as the house name is read — D-010 honoured structurally.

key_designs.name: Victory state(s) & D-010 reconciliation
key_designs.description: PRIMARY VICTORY: the Kurosawa are printed as a named, upper-rank house on the national/provincial mitate-banzuke — publicly reckoned a first-rank provincial house, the domain's leading family, restored AND surpassing the three-generations-ago grandeur. Recognition celebrates OVERALL influence & strength (all four pillars), not the product. SECONDARY/BRANCH victories let the player 'win their way' (a chart-topping MARTIAL rank as a house of famed retainers; a POLITICAL rank as the domain's administrative power; a PRESTIGE rank as a celebrated cultural patron-house) — each a distinct named final standing. D-010 KEPT: the protagonist is never on the chart, never hatamoto, never received by the shogun; the HOUSE rises and is ranked; he is its uncredited architect, topping out personally at karō-kaku (chief-retainer-equivalent — historically the highest a merit-risen non-born man could plausibly reach within a domain house). The protagonist's personal epilogue (true name Tahei, restored Kuzuhara, his people) stays human-scale, mirroring the partial-justice motif.

ideas: Render the banzuke as an actual in-game woodblock broadsheet UI (kanji columns, EAST/WEST, shrinking calligraphy down the ranks, compiler's-name margin) that re-prints each in-world season — every new edition is a chunky, satisfying reveal moment ('the spring banzuke is out'), turning the 家威 number into a collectible artifact the player anticipates.

Make the four pillars four visibly separate climbing bars PLUS a roll-up rank, so the player can deliberately lean a build (a martial house vs an administrative house vs a cultural house) and reach a differently-flavoured named final standing — replayability/identity without a reset.

Seed the great real houses (Maeda/Kaga, Shimazu/Satsuma, Date/Sendai) as static 'yokozuna' flavour entries at the chart's untouchable head, so the ceiling is something the player literally sees above them — the wall the powerful built, made diegetic.

Tie each Edo set-piece to a specific pillar and a specific protagonist domain (duel-of-houses→martial/combat, relief-crisis→economic+leadership, office-granted→political, inspector's-report→prestige) so the climax provably exercises combat, trades, jobs, AND leadership — directly satisfying the human's 'draw on ALL domains' ask.

Frame Tomita's defeat as a banzuke OVERTAKE (your name printed above theirs) rather than their destruction — out-maneuver, not kill — keeping the grounded 'status competitor' tone and the no-bloodbath authenticity; offer detente/forced-alliance/clean-eclipse resolutions.

Use a 'mae-zumo' (unranked bottom-margin names) start for the Kurosawa so the very first time the house name appears ANYWHERE on the chart is itself a milestone — a cheap, huge dopamine beat that costs one print line.

Let the player commission their OWN mitate-banzuke via the gesakusha/patronage thread (a prestige-pillar lever) — a grounded Edo practice (charts were privately compiled/sold) that lets the house buy fame legitimately through slow courtship of quality, never bribery.

Offer a 'banzuke of retainers' or 'banzuke of swordsmen' sub-chart (the real kengo/dojo-ranking genre — the Three Great Dojo of Edo era) as the martial pillar's public face, so martial standing has its own celebrated broadsheet, parallel to the houses' chart.
risks: D-010 STRAIN (flag for human): 'the house climbs a NATIONAL Edo ranking' and 'takes over the castle-town' is meaningfully more ambitious than the locked indirect/mediated ceiling. Even routed through the house (not the man), a provincial goshi house appearing on a *national/Edo-published* banzuke pushes plausibility. Three reconciliation options for the human: (A) KEEP D-010 as-is — make the chart explicitly a *provincial/regional* mitate-banzuke (a domain or multi-domain print, not a bakufu-national one), with the Kurosawa reaching a respectable provincial Maegashira rank and the national great-houses chart only glimpsed above; protagonist tops at karō-kaku. (B) LOOSEN D-010 slightly — allow a genuinely national mitate-banzuke (these existed for rich men nationwide) where a first-rank provincial house can appear low on the national chart, still house-level, protagonist still uncredited. (C) LOOSEN D-010 more — allow a modest personal house-name elevation/adoption for the protagonist as the architect's reward (strains the 'nobody stays a nobody' thesis; least recommended).

Trade de-weighting must be enforced in the BALANCE TABLES, not just prose — if the economic pillar's numbers dwarf the others, the game silently reverts to the farmer/trade-weighted design the human rejected. Cap economic at ~1/4 of roll-up weight and trade at ~1/3 of the economic pillar; generate the weights into docs/ so it's auditable (per the single-source-of-truth convention).

Four parallel pillars risk pacing mush (four slow bars crawling at once feels worse than one). Mitigate: stagger which pillars are 'live' per tier (martial+economic early-T4, political mid, prestige late) and let set-pieces deliver big chunky pillar jumps so progress is felt, not trickled.

A 'national ranking of houses' as a real Edo artifact is the weakest-grounded claim here — mitate-banzuke of *rich men/merchants/restaurants/disasters* are well-attested, but a banzuke specifically of samurai HOUSES is closer to inference. Keep it defensible by framing it as a parody/mitate broadsheet (the genre ranked anything) rather than an official bakufu register; have the §4/§5 research-harden verify before locking.

Scope/number-range blowup: a four-pillar chart across five tiers multiplies the balance surface. Confine the full banzuke to T4+ (T1–T3 keep the single hidden 家威 number) so the public chart is an endgame layer, not a system the whole game must support.
open_questions: Which D-010 reconciliation does the human want — (A) provincial-only chart keeping the ceiling intact, (B) national chart with a low house-level entry, or (C) loosen the ceiling for a personal elevation? (Recommend A or B; A is safest.)

Should the four pillars combine into ONE published rank, or should the house appear on FOUR separate banzuke (a martial chart, an economic chart, etc.) with no single combined number? (Separate charts are more grounded and give branch-victory clarity; one roll-up is cleaner UX.)
Exact pillar roll-up weights and the economic/trade caps — needs a balance pass and generation into docs/ to lock the de-trade-weighting numerically.

Is the protagonist's personal top rank locked at karō-kaku (chief-retainer-equivalent), and is that named on-screen, or kept as aspirational narration only (per the existing §1.13 guardrail that flagged karō/karō-kaku as 'aspirational narration only')?

Do the great real houses (Maeda/Shimazu/Date) appear by real name as chart flavour, or get fictionalized to match the project's 'generic-rural, no real place-names' guardrail (§1.13)? Real names add grounding but collide with that guardrail.
Does this redesign get its own superseding ADR (replacing the §1.11 'meibutsu celebrated in Edo' climax), and should the existing D-010 cap-the-ceiling ADR be amended in the same lock?

facets.facet: D — Beefed-up endgame goals, set-pieces & long-tail (multi-pillar, all-domains, no-reset)
facets.summary: The current endgame (PRD §1.11 / world-doc T5) is single-pillar — "the famous meibutsu is celebrated in Edo" — and still assumes the now-reversed D-004 reset. Per the redesign brief this is replaced with a MULTI-PILLAR climax: the house climbs a national, Edo-level mitate-BANZUKE that ranks goshi/merchant houses across four pillars (MARTIAL, ECONOMIC, POLITICAL/TERRITORIAL, PRESTIGE/CULTURAL), and the final main-quest arc + capstone set-pieces draw on the protagonist's COMBAT, JOBS, SKILLS, TRADES and LEADERSHIP together. Concretely I propose: (1) the banzuke becomes the visible T5 win-condition meter, fed by all four pillars (overall rank = a weighted combine the existing achievement-based House Influence already implies), so no single path — least of all trade — dominates; (2) the T3→T4 castle-town takeover ("peacefully or otherwise") is a multi-route "become the leading house" conquest — administrative appointment, economic leverage, marriage/alliance, daimyo's-favour, and martial-pressure/security routes — that politically eclipses the 2+ rival houses (Tomita + one more) WITHOUT open rebellion, honouring Edo authenticity; (3) the T4→T5 finale is a four-act multi-pillar push (the Touring-Inspector Survey as the judged capstone + the banzuke listing as the public climax + the lord's sankin-kotai as the mediated conduit + the mediated merit-notation in bakufu ink), each act demanding a different pillar; (4) one capstone CHALLENGE per pillar (the Hanzaki duel + a defended frontier for martial; the meibutsu-to-#1-in-its-category for economic; eclipsing the last rival house / holding a domain office for political; a recognized treatise or celebrated patronage for prestige); (5) the three side-threads pay off INTO the endgame as pillar fuel and partial-justice beats (the racket's osso petition is a political+prestige capstone with real personal risk on a chosen neck; Oharu/lost-child resolves at Kuzuhara as a prestige/closure beat; Kuranosuke/origin-father redemption is the trade-logistics capstone that crowns the economic pillar); and (6) a NO-RESET long tail: defend the banzuke top spot against rivals and a periodic re-ranking, optional grounded super-bosses (Hanzaki's roost, the brigand roost, a famine-winter siege), per-pillar mastery goals, and the fully-grown estate (E5) as a teaching/management sandbox via Tokujiro + recruited origin friends. Everything stays achievement/milestone-driven, grounded, lean (reuse existing cast/systems, abstract boards above T3), and honours the indirect ceiling: it is the HOUSE that is ranked and recognized nationally; the protagonist is its architect/right-hand, never personally ennobled past plausibility. I explicitly flag where "take over the castle-town" + "national ranking" strain the locked indirect-ceiling decision so the human can reconcile.

key_designs.name: The Edo Banzuke — multi-pillar national ranking IS the win condition (replaces meibutsu-celebrated-in-Edo)
key_designs.description: Promote the world-doc's T5 mitate-banzuke from a single product-fame chart into the endgame's spine. The climax is the HOUSE climbing a national, Edo-level banzuke (a woodblock ranking sheet of rural goshi/merchant houses, period-plausible as a popular gazetteer 'mitate' list, NOT an official bakufu register — which protects the ceiling). The sheet has FOUR pillar columns the house is rated in: MARTIAL (retainers/men-at-arms strength, victories, road/frontier security, rival-force defeats), ECONOMIC (lands reclaimed, treasury/creditworthiness, the meibutsu — ONE economic strand among several, never the whole sheet), POLITICAL/TERRITORIAL (villages→valleys→castle-town offices controlled, alliances, rivals eclipsed), and PRESTIGE/CULTURAL (reputation, patronage, notable deeds, lineage restored, recognition). Overall east/west rank = a weighted combine of the four; the player SEES four sub-bars feeding one rank and a few named rival houses above/below to overtake. Each pillar accrues only achievement/milestone-style (a won defence, a sealed alliance, a graded meibutsu lot, a recognized treatise) — the existing achievement-based Influence model, just split into four legible columns. De-trade-weighting is enforced by the combine: a trade-maxed house with weak martial/political/prestige columns sits MID-sheet, so the player is pulled to grow all four in parallel.

key_designs.name: The Castle-Town Takeover (T3→T4) — 'become the leading house, peacefully or otherwise' across multiple routes
key_designs.description: Reframe T4 from 'be reckoned with by officials' into an honest power-conquest: the Kurosawa rise to be the DOMINANT/leading house of the castle-town & domain seat, eclipsing the 2+ rival samurai houses (Tomita + a second incumbent leading house, e.g. an entrenched office-holding house) — never by storming a castle (impossible for a goshi house under the bakufu; flagged as the authenticity guardrail), but by seizing the key domain offices, administration, and recognition. FIVE parallel routes, each a different pillar so no one path dominates: (a) ADMINISTRATIVE — earn and hold a real domain office through the daikan's chain (kanjo-kata recording → an appointed sub-office), the cleanest political route; (b) ECONOMIC LEVERAGE — become the domain's indispensable creditor/supplier (Marutaya finance + the meibutsu), so officials cannot rule without the house; (c) MARRIAGE/ALLIANCE — the nakodo brokerage ties the house to a better-placed line or neutralizes a rival; (d) DAIMYO'S FAVOUR / political dethroning — out-perform rivals at the touring-inspector survey and in society so the daimyo's men prefer the Kurosawa, demoting the incumbent leading house; (e) ASSERTIVE/MARTIAL PRESSURE & SECURITY — within Edo-plausible bounds: the house's men-at-arms become the de-facto security of the roads/frontier the daikan relies on, defeat rivals' retainers in framed clashes/duels and break the racket's enforcer arm (Hanzaki/bandits), so martial strength is LEVERAGE and indispensability, not rebellion. The player can win T4 on any 2-3 routes; doing all of them makes the eclipse total. 'Dethroning' the rivals = out-supplying/out-arbitrating/out-performing them until they take detente, forced alliance, or clean commercial+social defeat (Tomita is never killed).

key_designs.name: The four-act multi-pillar finale (T4→T5) — every act demands a different domain
key_designs.description: The final main-quest arc is a four-act push to the top of the banzuke, deliberately spread across all the protagonist's domains so the climax can't be brute-forced on one pillar. ACT 1 — THE FRONTIER SECURED (MARTIAL/LEADERSHIP): the protagonist, now captain of retainers (R12), leads the men-at-arms to break the brigand/Hanzaki roost in the high pass and stand the climactic SIEGE/RAID-DEFENCE of the estate — recorded martial service that fills the martial column and removes the racket's enforcer arm (dent-not-destroy on failure, never a wipe). ACT 2 — THE PETITION & THE ECLIPSE (POLITICAL/SKILLS): the osso evidence-petition against the racket is mounted (drawing on skills — ledgers, surveying, letters, the gathered evidence from every network) and the last incumbent rival house is politically eclipsed via offices + alliance; partial justice — the daikan answers, the Edo laundering factor is glimpsed but untouchable. ACT 3 — THE MEIBUTSU & THE PURSE (ECONOMIC/TRADES): the graded meibutsu reaches its top category-rank in Nihonbashi and the restructured debt becomes national creditworthiness — the economic column, one strong pillar but NOT the whole climax. ACT 4 — THE SURVEY & THE LISTING (PRESTIGE + COMBINE): the Touring-Inspector Survey judges the whole house (all four pillars become 'the answer to one exam'), a favourable report travels UP through the lord, and the house's name appears and then CLIMBS the Edo banzuke to a leading rank — the mediated merit-notation in bakufu ink delivered DOWN through Konoe and Lord Munenori. The protagonist stands at the back of every recognition beat: the HOUSE rises, he is its architect.

key_designs.name: One capstone challenge per pillar + the side-threads as their emotional payloads
key_designs.description: Each pillar gets a discrete, hard, optional-but-iconic capstone challenge so 'everything the MC does matters' and each domain has a true mountaintop. MARTIAL: the Hanzaki DUEL won at last (survived-not-won for the whole game, finally beatable on labour-built endurance) + a frontier held through a famine-winter. ECONOMIC/TRADES: the meibutsu graded to #1 in its category AND the Kuranosuke/origin-father redemption — his debt bought back with resources the SON earned, crowning the trade-logistics pillar (the origin caravan/route becomes the supply chain that carries the meibutsu to Edo). POLITICAL/TERRITORIAL: the racket's osso petition won to its partial-justice limit (real personal risk on a chosen neck — recommend a gimin-style ally or the protagonist, reconciled with the partial-justice rule) + the last rival house eclipsed/allied. PRESTIGE/CULTURAL: a recognized treatise on the house's agronomy/water-works (the Kuzuhara embankment written up) OR a celebrated patronage, PLUS the Oharu/lost-child resolution at Kuzuhara — the living grown Tama freed and self-determining, the named drowned, the cover-up surfaced (costly, incomplete, she may not forgive). The three locked side-threads (lost-child, origin/family, the racket) thus each pay off INTO a different endgame pillar rather than fizzling — fulfilling 'everything matters' while keeping partial justice and the indirect ceiling.

key_designs.name: No-reset long tail — defend the top, optional super-bosses, per-pillar mastery, the estate as sandbox
key_designs.description: With NO reset (D-004 reversed), the post-climax long tail keeps the genre's loop without betraying canon. (1) DEFEND THE TOP SPOT: the banzuke RE-RANKS on a periodic cadence (seasonal/biennial, tied to the sankin-kotai heartbeat) and rivals push back, so holding a leading rank is an ongoing achievement-driven goal — slip on any pillar and a rival climbs above you, recoverable, never a wipe. (2) OPTIONAL GROUNDED SUPER-BOSSES: not magical raid bosses but the hardest grounded fights — Hanzaki's full roost at max difficulty, a coordinated multi-house bandit alliance hitting your branch holdings, the famine-winter siege — each repeatable/escalating and gated on labour-built conditioning, honouring no-hidden-edge. (3) PER-PILLAR MASTERY GOALS: max each weapon/labour/craft skill line, complete the bestiary, grade every meibutsu variant, reclaim every plot, secure every road, restore every named drowned — long lists of small achievement-driven dopamine that each tick a pillar. (4) THE ESTATE (E5) AS SANDBOX: the fully-grown estate becomes a teaching/management playground — Tokujiro and recruited origin friends are now taught 'from zero' (re-homing the old reset's teaching layer onto NPCs, per locked direction), the player tunes the standing patrol/garrison, branch holdings, and meibutsu quarter as a living machine. All long-tail stays lean by reusing existing systems (combat tables, idle producers, the banzuke meter) — no new content mountains, just escalation/mastery over what's built.

key_designs.name: Honouring the indirect/mediated ceiling — and where the new ambition strains it (flagged for the human)
key_designs.description: The brief's bigger 'take over the castle-town' + 'national ranking' is reconciled by keeping the HOUSE as what rises, is ranked, and is recognized — the protagonist is its architect/right-hand (caps at R14, never hatamoto, never received by the shogun, no rags-to-daimyo). Mechanisms that preserve this: the banzuke is a POPULAR gazetteer ranking of houses (cultural fame), NOT an official bakufu status grant; the castle-town 'takeover' = becoming the leading/dominant house and seizing domain (han-level) offices/administration, NOT bakufu rank and NOT military conquest; all Edo-side recognition routes DOWN through the rusui Konoe and Lord Munenori (the protagonist provisions the man who is seen, never is seen). EXPLICIT STRAIN POINTS to reconcile (D-010): (i) the protagonist personally HOLDING a domain office as the house's right-hand edges past 'never personally elevated' — option A: the office is held in the HOUSE's/lord's name with the protagonist as deputy/agent (keeps D-010); option B: loosen D-010 to allow a modest personal han-office (bigger personal ascension). (ii) 'eclipsing rival samurai houses' to become the leading house is a larger house-ascension than 'restore and surpass three-generations-ago grandeur' implied — option A: keep it as leading-house-of-the-DOMAIN only, with the national banzuke being fame not power (keeps the honest wall); option B: loosen toward a genuinely larger house ascension. (iii) the mediated merit-notation in bakufu ink (house name in an official register) is the heaviest beat — keep it strictly DELIVERED-down-through-the-lord (recommended) to hold the wall. Recommendation: keep D-010 as-is with house-level ranking + house-held offices; present option B explicitly so the human can choose a bigger ascension if desired.

ideas: Make the four banzuke pillar sub-bars VISIBLE late-game as the literal endgame progress UI: four columns (martial/economic/political/prestige) feeding one east/west rank, with 2-3 named rival houses shown directly above and below the Kurosawa to create concrete 'overtake that house' goals — the most legible single win-condition display in the game.

Enforce de-trade-weighting numerically: the overall-rank combine should penalize lopsidedness (e.g. weakest pillar is weighted heaviest, or the combine is closer to a min/geometric-mean than a sum) so a trade-maxed, martial/political-weak house literally cannot reach a leading rank — making 'grow all four in parallel' the only path up.

Add a SECOND incumbent leading house beside Tomita for the castle-town takeover so 'eclipse the 2+ rival houses' is concrete: Tomita = the upstart commercial rival (already canon), plus an entrenched office-holding old house = the political incumbent you dethrone administratively. Two rivals, two flavours of eclipse.

Give the castle-town takeover a route-tracker: the player picks/discovers which of the five routes (administrative / economic-leverage / marriage / daimyo's-favour / martial-pressure) they pursue; winning any 2-3 takes T4; the unused routes remain available as long-tail completionist goals — replayability without a reset.

Tie the long-tail banzuke RE-RANK to the existing sankin-kotai biennial heartbeat so the upper tier already has a clock: every attendance cycle the sheet re-ranks, rivals push, and the player must have kept all four pillars warm — a natural, grounded 'defend the top' loop.

Make the Touring-Inspector Survey the mechanical 'exam' that READS all four pillars at once: the inspector weighs fields (economic), order/security (martial), offices/administration (political), and the people's condition + meibutsu + deeds (prestige) — so the capstone literally scores every domain the MC built, and a neglected pillar visibly drags the report (recoverable dent, never a wipe).

Route each side-thread's payoff into a DIFFERENT pillar so none is redundant: origin-father Kuranosuke → economic/trades capstone (the supply chain that carries the meibutsu); racket/osso petition → political+prestige capstone (partial justice); Oharu/lost-child → prestige/closure capstone. 'Everything the MC does matters' becomes structurally true.

For the partial-justice osso petition, surface the 'whose neck' choice as a real late-game decision (protagonist / loyal ally / a gimin-style martyr NPC) with the most affecting option being genuine personal risk — reconciled with the partial-justice guardrail by keeping the win PARTIAL (daikan answers, Edo factor escapes) regardless of who risks the neck.

Super-bosses as grounded escalation, not magic: a max-difficulty Hanzaki roost, a multi-house bandit alliance raiding branch holdings, and a famine-winter siege — each repeatable/escalating, gated on labour-built conditioning, dropping the game's best FOUND gear and the heaviest martial-pillar deeds, so combat stays a first-class endgame pillar.

Re-home the old reset's teaching/management layer fully onto the E5 sandbox: Tokujiro and recruited origin friends are taught 'from zero' as a visible mirror of the protagonist's own climb, and the player tunes garrison/holdings/meibutsu as a living machine — the long tail's 'what keeps it engaging' answer, lean because it reuses existing producer/combat systems.

Keep upper tiers LEAN per the world-doc scope guard: render region/domain/Edo and the banzuke as abstract holdings/correspondence/ranking BOARDS rather than walkable maps (especially never-visited Osaka and arm's-length Edo), so the beefed-up endgame adds depth and goals without a content-art mountain.

Add per-pillar mastery achievement lists (max every weapon/labour/craft line, complete the bestiary, grade every meibutsu variant, secure every road, name every drowned) as small achievement-driven dopamine that each tick a pillar's column even post-climax — the incremental long tail without any reset.
risks: The multi-pillar banzuke + four-act finale + five takeover routes + four capstone challenges is a large content/balance surface that directly triggers canon's locked scope-explosion guardrail (§1.13) and the 'start lean, don't over-build' memory; this is an endgame DESIGN, not v1 scope — must be staged into the roadmap (v1 = Tiers 1-2; this is the T4-T5 capstone), or it balloons the build.

D-010 / indirect-ceiling STRAIN: 'take over the castle-town' + 'national ranking' + a protagonist holding/agenting domain offices are genuinely more ambitious than the locked 'indirect/mediated, never personally ennobled' ceiling. This needs an explicit human reconciliation (keep D-010 with house-level ranking + house-held offices, OR loosen D-010) before authoring — flagged in key-designs but unresolved.

De-trade-weighting can over-correct: if the pillar combine is too min-weighted, a player who genuinely enjoys the trade/economic loop is punished for specializing; tune so all four pillars are required for the TOP rank but specialization still progresses (the brief wants trade as one pillar among several, not trade made weak).

The osso 'whose neck' personal-risk option risks contradicting both the indirect ceiling (if the protagonist's risk reads as personal heroic elevation) and partial-justice (if the win feels clean) — the win must stay partial and the risk must not translate into personal ennoblement.

The combat-as-endgame-pillar (siege, super-bosses, Hanzaki) risks tilting the back-half away from the locked 'peaceful labour is the dominant texture' feel (the combat doc's verifier already flagged this); the endgame should keep labour/trades/skills pillars equally weighted in time, not become a combat finale.

The banzuke as 'national ranking' risks reading as bakufu-official status (breaking the ceiling) unless firmly framed as a POPULAR gazetteer/mitate fame ranking — the framing is load-bearing and easy to get wrong.

Several payoffs depend on the still-OPEN meibutsu-product decision and on ADRs not yet written (D-004 reversal, D-007 combat-earns-standing, the indirect-ceiling ADR); authoring the economic/prestige capstones before the meibutsu and these ADRs are locked is 'designing on sand' (per the world-doc verifier).

Periodic banzuke re-ranking as 'defend the top' could feel like a soft punishment/treadmill if rivals can knock you down too easily — must stay recoverable, never a wipe, and never force grind to merely stay put (keep it a 'keep all pillars warm' nudge, not a decay tax).
open_questions: Reconcile the indirect ceiling: keep D-010 as-is (house is ranked/recognized; offices held in the house's/lord's name with the protagonist as deputy-agent; banzuke = fame not bakufu status), OR loosen D-010 to allow a bigger personal/house ascension (a modest personal han-office, a genuinely larger house rise)? This is the load-bearing human decision the whole endgame hinges on.

Exact pillar-combine formula and weights: how do MARTIAL / ECONOMIC / POLITICAL / PRESTIGE combine into overall banzuke rank, and how min-weighted is it (enough to force all four for the top rank, without punishing enjoyable specialization)? Defer to the balance facet but the SHAPE must be locked.
Is the banzuke strictly a popular gazetteer 'mitate' fame ranking (recommended, protects the ceiling) or does it carry any official/administrative weight? Confirm it never reads as bakufu status.
Who/what is the SECOND rival leading house beside Tomita (the political incumbent the house dethrones administratively), and does adding it fit the lean-cast guard or overload the roster?

Which of the five castle-town-takeover routes are SPINE-minimal (how many must a player win to take T4) vs optional accelerants/completionist long-tail? Recommend 'any 2-3 takes T4', unused routes are long-tail — confirm.

The osso petition's 'whose neck is on the line' (protagonist / loyal ally / gimin-style martyr) and its timing (partial T4 redress, untouchable Edo apex glimpsed at T5) — reconcile with both partial-justice and the indirect ceiling.
Does the protagonist's personal rank stay HARD-capped at R14 while the house's banzuke rank keeps climbing post-R14 (recommended), confirming personal vs house ascension are decoupled?

Long-tail re-rank cadence and stakes: how often does the banzuke re-rank (seasonal vs the biennial sankin-kotai heartbeat), how hard do rivals push, and is slipping always cheaply recoverable (must honour 'never a wipe')?

Which product is the canonical meibutsu (still OPEN in canon) — it gates the economic/prestige capstones, the Nihonbashi category, and the banzuke product strand; lock before authoring the T4/T5 payoff nodes.

Pacing: do the three side-threads (lost-child, origin-father, racket) all CLIMAX at the T4-T5 finale alongside the banzuke, or resolve earlier (T3-4) with T5 as a quieter restoration victory lap? (Open per §1.14.) Recommend they pay off INTO the finale as pillar fuel, but confirm.

Time-budget split across the four pillars in the endgame so peaceful labour/trades/skills stay co-dominant with martial (target e.g. combat ≤~40% of endgame activity), honouring the locked 'peaceful labour is the dominant texture' pillar.
Confirm this whole multi-pillar endgame is explicitly a T4-T5 ROADMAP capstone (post-v1), not v1 scope, so it doesn't trip the lean-v1 / don't-over-build guardrails.
